verses.esv: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
verses.kjv: THE book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
verses.bsb: This is the record of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham:

verses.esv: Abraham was the father of Isaac, and Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers,
verses.kjv: Abraham begat Isaac; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat Judas and his brethren;
verses.bsb: Abraham was the father of Isaac, Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers.

verses.esv: and Judah the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, and Perez the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Ram,
verses.kjv: And Judas begat Phares and Zara of Thamar; and Phares begat Esrom; and Esrom begat Aram;
verses.bsb: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, Perez the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Ram.

verses.esv: and Ram the father of Amminadab, and Amminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon,
verses.kjv: And Aram begat Aminadab; and Aminadab begat Naasson; and Naasson begat Salmon;
verses.bsb: Ram was the father of Amminadab, Amminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon.

verses.esv: and Salmon the father of Boaz by Rahab, and Boaz the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed the father of Jesse,
verses.kjv: And Salmon begat Booz of Rachab; and Booz begat Obed of Ruth; and Obed begat Jesse;
verses.bsb: Salmon was the father of Boaz by Rahab, Boaz the father of Obed by Ruth, Obed the father of Jesse,

verses.esv: and Jesse the father of David the king. And David was the father of Solomon by the wife of Uriah,
verses.kjv: And Jesse begat David the king; and David the king begat Solomon of her that had been the wife of Urias;
verses.bsb: and Jesse the father of David the king.   Next:  David was the father of Solomon by Uriah’s wife,

verses.esv: and Solomon the father of Rehoboam, and Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asaph,
verses.kjv: And Solomon begat Roboam; and Roboam begat Abia; and Abia begat Asa;
verses.bsb: Solomon the father of Rehoboam, Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asa.

verses.esv: and Asaph the father of Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah,
verses.kjv: And Asa begat Josaphat; and Josaphat begat Joram; and Joram begat Ozias;
verses.bsb: Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah.

verses.esv: and Uzziah the father of Jotham, and Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
verses.kjv: And Ozias begat Joatham; and Joatham begat Achaz; and Achaz begat Ezekias;
verses.bsb: Uzziah was the father of Jotham, Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah.

verses.esv: and Hezekiah the father of Manasseh, and Manasseh the father of Amos, and Amos the father of Josiah,
verses.kjv: And Ezekias begat Manasses; and Manasses begat Amon; and Amon begat Josias;
verses.bsb: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh, Manasseh the father of Amon, Amon the father of Josiah,

verses.esv: and Josiah the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
verses.kjv: And Josias begat Jechonias and his brethren, about the time they were carried away to Babylon:
verses.bsb: and Josiah the father of Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon.

verses.esv: And after the deportation to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel, and Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel,
verses.kjv: And after they were brought to Babylon, Jechonias begat Salathiel; and Salathiel begat Zorobabel;
verses.bsb: After the exile to Babylon:  Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel, Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel,

verses.esv: and Zerubbabel the father of Abiud, and Abiud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor,
verses.kjv: And Zorobabel begat Abiud; and Abiud begat Eliakim; and Eliakim begat Azor;
verses.bsb: Zerubbabel the father of Abiud, Abiud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor.

verses.esv: and Azor the father of Zadok, and Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud,
verses.kjv: And Azor begat Sadoc; and Sadoc begat Achim; and Achim begat Eliud;
verses.bsb: Azor was the father of Zadok, Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud.

verses.esv: and Eliud the father of Eleazar, and Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob,
verses.kjv: And Eliud begat Eleazar; and Eleazar begat Matthan; and Matthan begat Jacob;
verses.bsb: Eliud was the father of Eleazar, Eleazar the father of Matthan, Matthan the father of Jacob,

verses.esv: and Jacob the father of Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom Jesus was born, who is called Christ.
verses.kjv: And Jacob begat Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.
verses.bsb: and Jacob the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.

verses.esv: So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations, and from David to the deportation to Babylon fourteen generations, and from the deportation to Babylon to the Christ fourteen generations.
verses.kjv: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David until the carrying away into Babylon are fourteen generations; and from the carrying away into Babylon unto Christ are fourteen generations.
verses.bsb: In all, then, there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen from the exile to the Christ.

verses.esv: Now the birth of Jesus Christ took place in this way. When his mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph, before they came together she was found to be with child from the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now the birth of Jesus Christ was on this wise: When as his mother Mary was espoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with child of the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ came about: His mother Mary was pledged in marriage to Joseph, but before they came together, she was found to be with child through the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: And her husband Joseph, being a just man and unwilling to put her to shame, resolved to divorce her quietly.
verses.kjv: Then Joseph her husband, being a just man, and not willing to make her a publick example, was minded to put her away privily.
verses.bsb: Because Joseph her husband was a righteous man and was unwilling to disgrace her publicly, he resolved to divorce her quietly.

verses.esv: But as he considered these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, “Joseph, son of David, do not fear to take Mary as your wife, for that which is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: But while he thought on these things, behold, the angel of the Lord appeared unto him in a dream, saying, Joseph, thou son of David, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife: for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: But after he had pondered these things, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to embrace Mary as your wife, for the One conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: She will bear a son, and you shall call his name Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.”
verses.kjv: And she shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his name JESUS: for he shall save his people from their sins.
verses.bsb: She will give birth to a Son, and you are to give Him the name Jesus, because He will save His people from their sins.”

verses.esv: All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had spoken by the prophet:
verses.kjv: Now all this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the prophet, saying,
verses.bsb: All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had said through the prophet:

verses.esv: “Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and they shall call his name Immanuel” (which means, God with us).
verses.kjv: Behold, a virgin shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel, which being interpreted is, God with us.
verses.bsb: “Behold, the virgin will be with child and will give birth to a son, and they will call Him Immanuel” (which means, “God with us”).

verses.esv: When Joseph woke from sleep, he did as the angel of the Lord commanded him: he took his wife,
verses.kjv: Then Joseph being raised from sleep did as the angel of the Lord had bidden him, and took unto him his wife:
verses.bsb: When Joseph woke up, he did as the angel of the Lord had commanded him, and embraced Mary as his wife.

verses.esv: but knew her not until she had given birth to a son. And he called his name Jesus.
verses.kjv: And knew her not till she had brought forth her firstborn son: and he called his name JESUS.
verses.bsb: But he had no union with her until she gave birth to a Son. And he gave Him the name Jesus.

verses.esv: Now after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of Herod the king, behold, wise men from the east came to Jerusalem,
verses.kjv: NOW when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judæa in the days of Herod the king, behold, there came wise men from the east to Jerusalem,
verses.bsb: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod, Magi from the east arrived in Jerusalem,

verses.esv: saying, “Where is he who has been born king of the Jews? For we saw his star when it rose and have come to worship him.”
verses.kjv: Saying, Where is he that is born King of the Jews? for we have seen his star in the east, and are come to worship him.
verses.bsb: asking, “Where is the One who has been born King of the Jews? We saw His star in the east and have come to worship Him.”

verses.esv: When Herod the king heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him;
verses.kjv: When Herod the king had heard these things, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
verses.bsb: When King Herod heard this, he was disturbed, and all Jerusalem with him.

verses.esv: and assembling all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Christ was to be born.
verses.kjv: And when he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people together, he demanded of them where Christ should be born.
verses.bsb: And when he had assembled all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he asked them where the Christ was to be born.

verses.esv: They told him, “In Bethlehem of Judea, for so it is written by the prophet:
verses.kjv: And they said unto him, In Bethlehem of Judæa: for thus it is written by the prophet,
verses.bsb: “In Bethlehem in Judea,” they replied, “for this is what the prophet has written:

verses.esv: “‘And you, O Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you shall come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.’”
verses.kjv: And thou Bethlehem, in the land of Juda, art not the least among the princes of Juda: for out of thee shall come a Governor, that shall rule my people Israel.
verses.bsb: ‘But you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah, for out of you will come a ruler who will be the shepherd of My people Israel.’”

verses.esv: Then Herod summoned the wise men secretly and ascertained from them what time the star had appeared.
verses.kjv: Then Herod, when he had privily called the wise men, inquired of them diligently what time the star appeared.
verses.bsb: Then Herod called the Magi secretly and learned from them the exact time the star had appeared.

verses.esv: And he sent them to Bethlehem, saying, “Go and search diligently for the child, and when you have found him, bring me word, that I too may come and worship him.”
verses.kjv: And he sent them to Bethlehem, and said, Go and search diligently for the young child; and when ye have found him, bring me word again, that I may come and worship him also.
verses.bsb: And sending them to Bethlehem, he said: “Go and search carefully for the Child, and when you find Him, report to me, so that I too may go and worship Him.”

verses.esv: After listening to the king, they went on their way. And behold, the star that they had seen when it rose went before them until it came to rest over the place where the child was.
verses.kjv: When they had heard the king, they departed; and, lo, the star, which they saw in the east, went before them, till it came and stood over where the young child was.
verses.bsb: After they had heard the king, they went on their way, and the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the Child was.

verses.esv: When they saw the star, they rejoiced exceedingly with great joy.
verses.kjv: When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceeding great joy.
verses.bsb: When they saw the star, they rejoiced with great delight.

verses.esv: And going into the house, they saw the child with Mary his mother, and they fell down and worshiped him. Then, opening their treasures, they offered him gifts, gold and frankincense and myrrh.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when they were come into the house, they saw the young child with Mary his mother, and fell down, and worshipped him: and when they had opened their treasures, they presented unto him gifts; gold, and frankincense, and myrrh.
verses.bsb: On coming to the house, they saw the Child with His mother Mary, and they fell down and worshiped Him. Then they opened their treasures and presented Him with gifts of gold and frankincense and myrrh.

verses.esv: And being warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they departed to their own country by another way.
verses.kjv: And being warned of God in a dream that they should not return to Herod, they departed into their own country another way.
verses.bsb: And having been warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they withdrew to their country by another route.

verses.esv: Now when they had departed, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Rise, take the child and his mother, and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you, for Herod is about to search for the child, to destroy him.”
verses.kjv: And when they were departed, behold, the angel of the Lord appeareth to Joseph in a dream, saying, Arise, and take the young child and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and be thou there until I bring thee word: for Herod will seek the young child to destroy him.
verses.bsb: When the Magi had gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream. “Get up!” he said. “Take the Child and His mother and flee to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod is going to search for the Child to kill Him.”

verses.esv: And he rose and took the child and his mother by night and departed to Egypt
verses.kjv: When he arose, he took the young child and his mother by night, and departed into Egypt:
verses.bsb: So he got up, took the Child and His mother by night, and withdrew to Egypt,

verses.esv: and remained there until the death of Herod. This was to fulfill what the Lord had spoken by the prophet, “Out of Egypt I called my son.”
verses.kjv: And was there until the death of Herod: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the prophet, saying, Out of Egypt have I called my son.
verses.bsb: where he stayed until the death of Herod. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: “Out of Egypt I called My Son.”

verses.esv: Then Herod, when he saw that he had been tricked by the wise men, became furious, and he sent and killed all the male children in Bethlehem and in all that region who were two years old or under, according to the time that he had ascertained from the wise men.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Herod, when he saw that he was mocked of the wise men, was exceeding wroth, and sent forth, and slew all the children that were in Bethlehem, and in all the coasts thereof, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had diligently inquired of the wise men.
verses.bsb: When Herod saw that he had been outwitted by the Magi, he was filled with rage. Sending orders, he put to death all the boys in Bethlehem and its vicinity who were two years old and under, according to the time he had learned from the Magi.

verses.esv: Then was fulfilled what was spoken by the prophet Jeremiah:
verses.kjv: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremy the prophet, saying,
verses.bsb: Then what was spoken through the prophet Jeremiah was fulfilled:

verses.esv: “A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and loud lamentation, Rachel weeping for her children; she refused to be comforted, because they are no more.”
verses.kjv: In Rama was there a voice heard, lamentation, and weeping, and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children, and would not be comforted, because they are not.
verses.bsb: “A voice is heard in Ramah, weeping and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children, and refusing to be comforted, because they are no more.”

verses.esv: But when Herod died, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt,
verses.kjv: ¶ But when Herod was dead, behold, an angel of the Lord appeareth in a dream to Joseph in Egypt,
verses.bsb: After Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.

verses.esv: saying, “Rise, take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel, for those who sought the child's life are dead.”
verses.kjv: Saying, Arise, and take the young child and his mother, and go into the land of Israel: for they are dead which sought the young child’s life.
verses.bsb: “Get up!” he said. “Take the Child and His mother and go to the land of Israel, for those seeking the Child’s life are now dead.”

verses.esv: And he rose and took the child and his mother and went to the land of Israel.
verses.kjv: And he arose, and took the young child and his mother, and came into the land of Israel.
verses.bsb: So Joseph got up, took the Child and His mother, and went to the land of Israel.

verses.esv: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there, and being warned in a dream he withdrew to the district of Galilee.
verses.kjv: But when he heard that Archelaus did reign in Judæa in the room of his father Herod, he was afraid to go thither: notwithstanding, being warned of God in a dream, he turned aside into the parts of Galilee:
verses.bsb: But when he learned that Archelaus was reigning in Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. And having been warned in a dream, he withdrew to the district of Galilee,

verses.esv: And he went and lived in a city called Nazareth, so that what was spoken by the prophets might be fulfilled, that he would be called a Nazarene.
verses.kjv: And he came and dwelt in a city called Nazareth: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophets, He shall be called a Nazarene.
verses.bsb: and he went and lived in a town called Nazareth. So was fulfilled what was spoken through the prophets: “He will be called a Nazarene.”

verses.esv: In those days John the Baptist came preaching in the wilderness of Judea,
verses.kjv: IN those days came John the Baptist, preaching in the wilderness of Judæa,
verses.bsb: In those days John the Baptist came, preaching in the wilderness of Judea

verses.esv: “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”
verses.kjv: And saying, Repent ye: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.
verses.bsb: and saying, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is near.”

verses.esv: For this is he who was spoken of by the prophet Isaiah when he said, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord; make his paths straight.’”
verses.kjv: For this is he that was spoken of by the prophet Esaias, saying, The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
verses.bsb: This is he who was spoken of through the prophet Isaiah:  “A voice of one calling in the wilderness, ‘Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for Him.’”

verses.esv: Now John wore a garment of camel's hair and a leather belt around his waist, and his food was locusts and wild honey.
verses.kjv: And the same John had his raiment of camel’s hair, and a leathern girdle about his loins; and his meat was locusts and wild honey.
verses.bsb: John wore a garment of camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.

verses.esv: Then Jerusalem and all Judea and all the region about the Jordan were going out to him,
verses.kjv: Then went out to him Jerusalem, and all Judæa, and all the region round about Jordan,
verses.bsb: People went out to him from Jerusalem and all Judea and the whole region around the Jordan.

verses.esv: and they were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
verses.kjv: And were baptized of him in Jordan, confessing their sins.
verses.bsb: Confessing their sins, they were baptized by him in the Jordan River.

verses.esv: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them, “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
verses.kjv: ¶ But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees come to his baptism, he said unto them, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
verses.bsb: But when John saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his place of baptism, he said to them, “You brood of vipers, who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?

verses.esv: Bear fruit in keeping with repentance.
verses.kjv: Bring forth therefore fruits meet for repentance:
verses.bsb: Produce fruit, then, in keeping with repentance.

verses.esv: And do not presume to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father,’ for I tell you, God is able from these stones to raise up children for Abraham.
verses.kjv: And think not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham to our father: for I say unto you, that God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.
verses.bsb: And do not presume to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father.’ For I tell you that out of these stones God can raise up children for Abraham.

verses.esv: Even now the axe is laid to the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
verses.kjv: And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees: therefore every tree which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.
verses.bsb: The axe lies ready at the root of the trees, and every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.

verses.esv: “I baptize you with water for repentance, but he who is coming after me is mightier than I, whose sandals I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
verses.kjv: I indeed baptize you with water unto repentance: but he that cometh after me is mightier than I, whose shoes I am not worthy to bear: he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost, and with fire:
verses.bsb: I baptize you with water for repentance, but after me will come One more powerful than I, whose sandals I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.

verses.esv: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will clear his threshing floor and gather his wheat into the barn, but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.”
verses.kjv: Whose fan is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and gather his wheat into the garner; but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.
verses.bsb: His winnowing fork is in His hand to clear His threshing floor and to gather His wheat into the barn; but He will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”

verses.esv: Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to John, to be baptized by him.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then cometh Jesus from Galilee to Jordan unto John, to be baptized of him.
verses.bsb: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John.

verses.esv: John would have prevented him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”
verses.kjv: But John forbad him, saying, I have need to be baptized of thee, and comest thou to me?
verses.bsb: But John tried to prevent Him, saying, “I need to be baptized by You, and do You come to me?”

verses.esv: But Jesus answered him, “Let it be so now, for thus it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he consented.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, Suffer it to be so now: for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness. Then he suffered him.
verses.bsb: “Let it be so now,” Jesus replied. “It is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness in this way.” Then John permitted Him.

verses.esv: And when Jesus was baptized, immediately he went up from the water, and behold, the heavens were opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and coming to rest on him;
verses.kjv: And Jesus, when he was baptized, went up straightway out of the water: and, lo, the heavens were opened unto him, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove, and lighting upon him:
verses.bsb: As soon as Jesus was baptized, He went up out of the water. Suddenly the heavens were opened, and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and resting on Him.

verses.esv: and behold, a voice from heaven said, “This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased.”
verses.kjv: And lo a voice from heaven, saying, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
verses.bsb: And a voice from heaven said, “This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased!”

verses.esv: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
verses.kjv: THEN was Jesus led up of the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted of the devil.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.

verses.esv: And after fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry.
verses.kjv: And when he had fasted forty days and forty nights, he was afterward an hungred.
verses.bsb: After fasting forty days and forty nights, He was hungry.

verses.esv: And the tempter came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become loaves of bread.”
verses.kjv: And when the tempter came to him, he said, If thou be the Son of God, command that these stones be made bread.
verses.bsb: The tempter came to Him and said, “If You are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.”

verses.esv: But he answered, “It is written, “‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.’”
verses.kjv: But he answered and said, It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.
verses.bsb: But Jesus answered, “It is written:  ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’”

verses.esv: Then the devil took him to the holy city and set him on the pinnacle of the temple
verses.kjv: Then the devil taketh him up into the holy city, and setteth him on a pinnacle of the temple,
verses.bsb: Then the devil took Him to the holy city and set Him on the pinnacle of the temple.

verses.esv: and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down, for it is written, “‘He will command his angels concerning you,’ and “‘On their hands they will bear you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.’”
verses.kjv: And saith unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down: for it is written, He shall give his angels charge concerning thee: and in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.
verses.bsb: “If You are the Son of God,” he said, “throw Yourself down. For it is written:  ‘He will command His angels concerning You, and they will lift You up in their hands, so that You will not strike Your foot against a stone.’”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “Again it is written, ‘You shall not put the Lord your God to the test.’”
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto him, It is written again, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’”

verses.esv: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
verses.kjv: Again, the devil taketh him up into an exceeding high mountain, and sheweth him all the kingdoms of the world, and the glory of them;
verses.bsb: Again, the devil took Him to a very high mountain and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.

verses.esv: And he said to him, “All these I will give you, if you will fall down and worship me.”
verses.kjv: And saith unto him, All these things will I give thee, if thou wilt fall down and worship me.
verses.bsb: “All this I will give You,” he said, “if You will fall down and worship me.”

verses.esv: Then Jesus said to him, “Be gone, Satan! For it is written, “‘You shall worship the Lord your God and him only shall you serve.’”
verses.kjv: Then saith Jesus unto him, Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.
verses.bsb: “Away from Me, Satan!” Jesus declared. “For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve Him only.’”

verses.esv: Then the devil left him, and behold, angels came and were ministering to him.
verses.kjv: Then the devil leaveth him, and, behold, angels came and ministered unto him.
verses.bsb: Then the devil left Him, and angels came and ministered to Him.

verses.esv: Now when he heard that John had been arrested, he withdrew into Galilee.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now when Jesus had heard that John was cast into prison, he departed into Galilee;
verses.bsb: When Jesus heard that John had been imprisoned, He withdrew to Galilee.

verses.esv: And leaving Nazareth he went and lived in Capernaum by the sea, in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali,
verses.kjv: And leaving Nazareth, he came and dwelt in Capernaum, which is upon the sea coast, in the borders of Zabulon and Nephthalim:
verses.bsb: Leaving Nazareth, He went and lived in Capernaum, which is by the sea in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali,

verses.esv: so that what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled:
verses.kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying,
verses.bsb: to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:

verses.esv: “The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, the way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles—
verses.kjv: The land of Zabulon, and the land of Nephthalim, by the way of the sea, beyond Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles;
verses.bsb: “Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, the Way of the Sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles—

verses.esv: the people dwelling in darkness have seen a great light, and for those dwelling in the region and shadow of death, on them a light has dawned.”
verses.kjv: The people which sat in darkness saw great light; and to them which sat in the region and shadow of death light is sprung up.
verses.bsb: the people living in darkness have seen a great light; on those living in the land of the shadow of death, a light has dawned.”

verses.esv: From that time Jesus began to preach, saying, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”
verses.kjv: ¶ From that time Jesus began to preach, and to say, Repent: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.
verses.bsb: From that time on Jesus began to preach, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is near.”

verses.esv: While walking by the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon (who is called Peter) and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus, walking by the sea of Galilee, saw two brethren, Simon called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
verses.bsb: As Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee, He saw two brothers, Simon called Peter and his brother Andrew. They were casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.”
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.
verses.bsb: “Come, follow Me,” Jesus said, “and I will make you fishers of men.”

verses.esv: Immediately they left their nets and followed him.
verses.kjv: And they straightway left their nets, and followed him.
verses.bsb: And at once they left their nets and followed Him.

verses.esv: And going on from there he saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets, and he called them.
verses.kjv: And going on from thence, he saw other two brethren, James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, in a ship with Zebedee their father, mending their nets; and he called them.
verses.bsb: Going on from there, He saw two other brothers, James son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with their father Zebedee, mending their nets. Jesus called them,

verses.esv: Immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
verses.kjv: And they immediately left the ship and their father, and followed him.
verses.bsb: and immediately they left the boat and their father and followed Him.

verses.esv: And he went throughout all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom and healing every disease and every affliction among the people.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus went about all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing all manner of sickness and all manner of disease among the people.
verses.bsb: Jesus went throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness among the people.

verses.esv: So his fame spread throughout all Syria, and they brought him all the sick, those afflicted with various diseases and pains, those oppressed by demons, those having seizures, and paralytics, and he healed them.
verses.kjv: And his fame went throughout all Syria: and they brought unto him all sick people that were taken with divers diseases and torments, and those which were possessed with devils, and those which were lunatick, and those that had the palsy; and he healed them.
verses.bsb: News about Him spread all over Syria, and people brought to Him all who were ill with various diseases, those suffering acute pain, the demon-possessed, those having seizures, and the paralyzed—and He healed them.

verses.esv: And great crowds followed him from Galilee and the Decapolis, and from Jerusalem and Judea, and from beyond the Jordan.
verses.kjv: And there followed him great multitudes of people from Galilee, and from Decapolis, and from Jerusalem, and from Judæa, and from beyond Jordan.
verses.bsb: The large crowds that followed Him came from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and beyond the Jordan.

verses.esv: Seeing the crowds, he went up on the mountain, and when he sat down, his disciples came to him.
verses.kjv: AND seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain: and when he was set, his disciples came unto him:
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw the crowds, He went up on the mountain and sat down. His disciples came to Him,

verses.esv: And he opened his mouth and taught them, saying:
verses.kjv: And he opened his mouth, and taught them, saying,
verses.bsb: and He began to teach them, saying:

verses.esv: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
verses.kjv: Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
verses.bsb: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

verses.esv: “Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.
verses.kjv: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.
verses.bsb: Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.

verses.esv: “Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.
verses.kjv: Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.
verses.bsb: Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.

verses.esv: “Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be satisfied.
verses.kjv: Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.
verses.bsb: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.

verses.esv: “Blessed are the merciful, for they shall receive mercy.
verses.kjv: Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.
verses.bsb: Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy.

verses.esv: “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.
verses.kjv: Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.
verses.bsb: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.

verses.esv: “Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God.
verses.kjv: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
verses.bsb: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called sons of God.

verses.esv: “Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness' sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
verses.kjv: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
verses.bsb: Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

verses.esv: “Blessed are you when others revile you and persecute you and utter all kinds of evil against you falsely on my account.
verses.kjv: Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake.
verses.bsb: Blessed are you when people insult you, persecute you, and falsely say all kinds of evil against you because of Me.

verses.esv: Rejoice and be glad, for your reward is great in heaven, for so they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
verses.kjv: Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven: for so persecuted they the prophets which were before you.
verses.bsb: Rejoice and be glad, because great is your reward in heaven; for in the same way they persecuted the prophets before you.

verses.esv: “You are the salt of the earth, but if salt has lost its taste, how shall its saltiness be restored? It is no longer good for anything except to be thrown out and trampled under people's feet.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye are the salt of the earth: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be salted? it is thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out, and to be trodden under foot of men.
verses.bsb: You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its savor, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled by men.

verses.esv: “You are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden.
verses.kjv: Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.
verses.bsb: You are the light of the world. A city on a hill cannot be hidden.

verses.esv: Nor do people light a lamp and put it under a basket, but on a stand, and it gives light to all in the house.
verses.kjv: Neither do men light a candle, and put it under a bushel, but on a candlestick; and it giveth light unto all that are in the house.
verses.bsb: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they set it on a stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house.

verses.esv: In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father who is in heaven.
verses.kjv: Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: In the same way, let your light shine before men, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.

verses.esv: “Do not think that I have come to abolish the Law or the Prophets; I have not come to abolish them but to fulfill them.
verses.kjv: ¶ Think not that I am come to destroy the law, or the prophets: I am not come to destroy, but to fulfil.
verses.bsb: Do not think that I have come to abolish the Law or the Prophets. I have not come to abolish them, but to fulfill them.

verses.esv: For truly, I say to you, until heaven and earth pass away, not an iota, not a dot, will pass from the Law until all is accomplished.
verses.kjv: For verily I say unto you, Till heaven and earth pass, one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the law, till all be fulfilled.
verses.bsb: For I tell you truly, until heaven and earth pass away, not a single jot, not a stroke of a pen, will disappear from the Law until everything is accomplished.

verses.esv: Therefore whoever relaxes one of the least of these commandments and teaches others to do the same will be called least in the kingdom of heaven, but whoever does them and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore shall break one of these least commandments, and shall teach men so, he shall be called the least in the kingdom of heaven: but whosoever shall do and teach them, the same shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
verses.bsb: So then, whoever breaks one of the least of these commandments and teaches others to do likewise will be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever practices and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.

verses.esv: For I tell you, unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
verses.kjv: For I say unto you, That except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven.
verses.bsb: For I tell you that unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.

verses.esv: “You have heard that it was said to those of old, ‘You shall not murder; and whoever murders will be liable to judgment.’
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not kill; and whosoever shall kill shall be in danger of the judgment:
verses.bsb: You have heard that it was said to the ancients, ‘Do not murder’ and ‘Anyone who murders will be subject to judgment.’

verses.esv: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with his brother will be liable to judgment; whoever insults his brother will be liable to the council; and whoever says, ‘You fool!’ will be liable to the hell of fire.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment: and whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the council: but whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that anyone who is angry with his brother will be subject to judgment. Again, anyone who says to his brother, ‘Raca,’ will be subject to the Sanhedrin. But anyone who says, ‘You fool!’ will be subject to the fire of hell.

verses.esv: So if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you,
verses.kjv: Therefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar, and there rememberest that thy brother hath ought against thee;
verses.bsb: So if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you,

verses.esv: leave your gift there before the altar and go. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.
verses.kjv: Leave there thy gift before the altar, and go thy way; first be reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift.
verses.bsb: leave your gift there before the altar. First go and be reconciled to your brother; then come and offer your gift.

verses.esv: Come to terms quickly with your accuser while you are going with him to court, lest your accuser hand you over to the judge, and the judge to the guard, and you be put in prison.
verses.kjv: Agree with thine adversary quickly, whiles thou art in the way with him; lest at any time the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.
verses.bsb: Reconcile quickly with your adversary, while you are still on the way to court. Otherwise, he may hand you over to the judge, and the judge may hand you over to the officer, and you may be thrown into prison.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, you will never get out until you have paid the last penny.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto thee, Thou shalt by no means come out thence, till thou hast paid the uttermost farthing.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, you will not get out until you have paid the last penny.

verses.esv: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall not commit adultery.’
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not commit adultery:
verses.bsb: You have heard that it was said, ‘Do not commit adultery.’

verses.esv: But I say to you that everyone who looks at a woman with lustful intent has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever looketh on a woman to lust after her hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that anyone who looks at a woman to lust after her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.

verses.esv: If your right eye causes you to sin, tear it out and throw it away. For it is better that you lose one of your members than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
verses.kjv: And if thy right eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.
verses.bsb: If your right eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.

verses.esv: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. For it is better that you lose one of your members than that your whole body go into hell.
verses.kjv: And if thy right hand offend thee, cut it off, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.
verses.bsb: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to depart into hell.

verses.esv: “It was also said, ‘Whoever divorces his wife, let him give her a certificate of divorce.’
verses.kjv: It hath been said, Whosoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorcement:
verses.bsb: It has also been said, ‘Whoever divorces his wife must give her a certificate of divorce.’

verses.esv: But I say to you that everyone who divorces his wife, except on the ground of sexual immorality, makes her commit adultery, and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever shall put away his wife, saving for the cause of fornication, causeth her to commit adultery: and whosoever shall marry her that is divorced committeth adultery.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that anyone who divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, brings adultery upon her. And he who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.

verses.esv: “Again you have heard that it was said to those of old, ‘You shall not swear falsely, but shall perform to the Lord what you have sworn.’
verses.kjv: ¶ Again, ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time, Thou shalt not forswear thyself, but shalt perform unto the Lord thine oaths:
verses.bsb: Again, you have heard that it was said to the ancients, ‘Do not break your oath, but fulfill your vows to the Lord.’

verses.esv: But I say to you, Do not take an oath at all, either by heaven, for it is the throne of God,
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, Swear not at all; neither by heaven; for it is God’s throne:
verses.bsb: But I tell you not to swear at all: either by heaven, for it is God’s throne;

verses.esv: or by the earth, for it is his footstool, or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
verses.kjv: Nor by the earth; for it is his footstool: neither by Jerusalem; for it is the city of the great King.
verses.bsb: or by the earth, for it is His footstool; or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.

verses.esv: And do not take an oath by your head, for you cannot make one hair white or black.
verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair white or black.
verses.bsb: Nor should you swear by your head, for you cannot make a single hair white or black.

verses.esv: Let what you say be simply ‘Yes’ or ‘No’; anything more than this comes from evil.
verses.kjv: But let your communication be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay: for whatsoever is more than these cometh of evil.
verses.bsb: Simply let your ‘Yes’ be ‘Yes,’ and your ‘No,’ ‘No.’ Anything more comes from the evil one.

verses.esv: “You have heard that it was said, ‘An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.’
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye have heard that it hath been said, An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth:
verses.bsb: You have heard that it was said, ‘Eye for eye and tooth for tooth.’

verses.esv: But I say to you, Do not resist the one who is evil. But if anyone slaps you on the right cheek, turn to him the other also.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That ye resist not evil: but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.
verses.bsb: But I tell you not to resist an evil person. If someone slaps you on your right cheek, turn to him the other also;

verses.esv: And if anyone would sue you and take your tunic, let him have your cloak as well.
verses.kjv: And if any man will sue thee at the law, and take away thy coat, let him have thy cloke also.
verses.bsb: if someone wants to sue you and take your tunic, let him have your cloak as well;

verses.esv: And if anyone forces you to go one mile, go with him two miles.
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall compel thee to go a mile, go with him twain.
verses.bsb: and if someone forces you to go one mile, go with him two miles.

verses.esv: Give to the one who begs from you, and do not refuse the one who would borrow from you.
verses.kjv: Give to him that asketh thee, and from him that would borrow of thee turn not thou away.
verses.bsb: Give to the one who asks you, and do not turn away from the one who wants to borrow from you.

verses.esv: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy.’
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye have heard that it hath been said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and hate thine enemy.
verses.bsb: You have heard that it was said, ‘Love your neighbor’ and ‘Hate your enemy.’

verses.esv: But I say to you, Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you;
verses.bsb: But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,

verses.esv: so that you may be sons of your Father who is in heaven. For he makes his sun rise on the evil and on the good, and sends rain on the just and on the unjust.
verses.kjv: That ye may be the children of your Father which is in heaven: for he maketh his sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.
verses.bsb: that you may be sons of your Father in heaven. He causes His sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.

verses.esv: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?
verses.kjv: For if ye love them which love you, what reward have ye? do not even the publicans the same?
verses.bsb: If you love those who love you, what reward will you get? Do not even tax collectors do the same?

verses.esv: And if you greet only your brothers, what more are you doing than others? Do not even the Gentiles do the same?
verses.kjv: And if ye salute your brethren only, what do ye more than others? do not even the publicans so?
verses.bsb: And if you greet only your brothers, what are you doing more than others? Do not even Gentiles do the same?

verses.esv: You therefore must be perfect, as your heavenly Father is perfect.
verses.kjv: Be ye therefore perfect, even as your Father which is in heaven is perfect.
verses.bsb: Be perfect, therefore, as your heavenly Father is perfect.

verses.esv: “Beware of practicing your righteousness before other people in order to be seen by them, for then you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
verses.kjv: TAKE heed that ye do not your alms before men, to be seen of them: otherwise ye have no reward of your Father which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: “Be careful not to perform your righteous acts before men to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven.

verses.esv: “Thus, when you give to the needy, sound no trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may be praised by others. Truly, I say to you, they have received their reward.
verses.kjv: Therefore when thou doest thine alms, do not sound a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have glory of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
verses.bsb: So when you give to the needy, do not sound a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honored by men. Truly I tell you, they already have their full reward.

verses.esv: But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
verses.kjv: But when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:
verses.bsb: But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,

verses.esv: so that your giving may be in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
verses.kjv: That thine alms may be in secret: and thy Father which seeth in secret himself shall reward thee openly.
verses.bsb: so that your giving may be in secret. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.

verses.esv: “And when you pray, you must not be like the hypocrites. For they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and at the street corners, that they may be seen by others. Truly, I say to you, they have received their reward.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when thou prayest, thou shalt not be as the hypocrites are: for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
verses.bsb: And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites. For they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by men. Truly I tell you, they already have their full reward.

verses.esv: But when you pray, go into your room and shut the door and pray to your Father who is in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
verses.kjv: But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy door, pray to thy Father which is in secret; and thy Father which seeth in secret shall reward thee openly.
verses.bsb: But when you pray, go into your inner room, shut your door, and pray to your Father, who is unseen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.

verses.esv: “And when you pray, do not heap up empty phrases as the Gentiles do, for they think that they will be heard for their many words.
verses.kjv: But when ye pray, use not vain repetitions, as the heathen do: for they think that they shall be heard for their much speaking.
verses.bsb: And when you pray, do not babble on like pagans, for they think that by their many words they will be heard.

verses.esv: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
verses.kjv: Be not ye therefore like unto them: for your Father knoweth what things ye have need of, before ye ask him.
verses.bsb: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask Him.

verses.esv: Pray then like this: “Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name.
verses.kjv: After this manner therefore pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name.
verses.bsb: So then, this is how you should pray:  ‘Our Father in heaven, hallowed be Your name.

verses.esv: Your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.
verses.kjv: Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven.
verses.bsb: Your kingdom come, Your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.

verses.esv: Give us this day our daily bread,
verses.kjv: Give us this day our daily bread.
verses.bsb: Give us this day our daily bread.

verses.esv: and forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.
verses.kjv: And forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.
verses.bsb: And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.

verses.esv: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.
verses.kjv: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil: For thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, for ever. Amen.
verses.bsb: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one.’

verses.esv: For if you forgive others their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you,
verses.kjv: For if ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you:
verses.bsb: For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.

verses.esv: but if you do not forgive others their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
verses.kjv: But if ye forgive not men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
verses.bsb: But if you do not forgive men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive yours.

verses.esv: “And when you fast, do not look gloomy like the hypocrites, for they disfigure their faces that their fasting may be seen by others. Truly, I say to you, they have received their reward.
verses.kjv: ¶ Moreover when ye fast, be not, as the hypocrites, of a sad countenance: for they disfigure their faces, that they may appear unto men to fast. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
verses.bsb: When you fast, do not be somber like the hypocrites, for they disfigure their faces to show men they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they already have their full reward.

verses.esv: But when you fast, anoint your head and wash your face,
verses.kjv: But thou, when thou fastest, anoint thine head, and wash thy face;
verses.bsb: But when you fast, anoint your head and wash your face,

verses.esv: that your fasting may not be seen by others but by your Father who is in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
verses.kjv: That thou appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father which is in secret: and thy Father, which seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
verses.bsb: so that your fasting will not be obvious to men, but only to your Father, who is unseen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.

verses.esv: “Do not lay up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy and where thieves break in and steal,
verses.kjv: ¶ Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves break through and steal:
verses.bsb: Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy, and where thieves break in and steal.

verses.esv: but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust destroys and where thieves do not break in and steal.
verses.kjv: But lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt, and where thieves do not break through nor steal:
verses.bsb: But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where moth and rust do not destroy, and where thieves do not break in and steal.

verses.esv: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
verses.kjv: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
verses.bsb: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.

verses.esv: “The eye is the lamp of the body. So, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light,
verses.kjv: The light of the body is the eye: if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body shall be full of light.
verses.bsb: The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are good, your whole body will be full of light.

verses.esv: but if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!
verses.kjv: But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is that darkness!
verses.bsb: But if your eyes are bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great is that darkness!

verses.esv: “No one can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and money.
verses.kjv: ¶ No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.
verses.bsb: No one can serve two masters: Either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.

verses.esv: “Therefore I tell you, do not be anxious about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink, nor about your body, what you will put on. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing?
verses.kjv: Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on. Is not the life more than meat, and the body than raiment?
verses.bsb: Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes?

verses.esv: Look at the birds of the air: they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of more value than they?
verses.kjv: Behold the fowls of the air: for they sow not, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are ye not much better than they?
verses.bsb: Look at the birds of the air: They do not sow or reap or gather into barns—and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they?

verses.esv: And which of you by being anxious can add a single hour to his span of life?
verses.kjv: Which of you by taking thought can add one cubit unto his stature?
verses.bsb: Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?

verses.esv: And why are you anxious about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin,
verses.kjv: And why take ye thought for raiment? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they toil not, neither do they spin:
verses.bsb: And why do you worry about clothes? Consider how the lilies of the field grow: They do not labor or spin.

verses.esv: yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
verses.kjv: And yet I say unto you, That even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
verses.bsb: Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his glory was adorned like one of these.

verses.esv: But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which today is alive and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will he not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?
verses.kjv: Wherefore, if God so clothe the grass of the field, which to day is, and to morrow is cast into the oven, shall he not much more clothe you, O ye of little faith?
verses.bsb: If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, will He not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?

verses.esv: Therefore do not be anxious, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’
verses.kjv: Therefore take no thought, saying, What shall we eat? or, What shall we drink? or, Wherewithal shall we be clothed?
verses.bsb: Therefore do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’

verses.esv: For the Gentiles seek after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them all.
verses.kjv: (For after all these things do the Gentiles seek:) for your heavenly Father knoweth that ye have need of all these things.
verses.bsb: For the Gentiles strive after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them.

verses.esv: But seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things will be added to you.
verses.kjv: But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.
verses.bsb: But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things will be added unto you.

verses.esv: “Therefore do not be anxious about tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Sufficient for the day is its own trouble.
verses.kjv: Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.
verses.bsb: Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Today has enough trouble of its own.

verses.esv: “Judge not, that you be not judged.
verses.kjv: JUDGE not, that ye be not judged.
verses.bsb: “Do not judge, or you will be judged.

verses.esv: For with the judgment you pronounce you will be judged, and with the measure you use it will be measured to you.
verses.kjv: For with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged: and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.
verses.bsb: For with the same judgment you pronounce, you will be judged; and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.

verses.esv: Why do you see the speck that is in your brother's eye, but do not notice the log that is in your own eye?
verses.kjv: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother’s eye, but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
verses.bsb: Why do you look at the speck in your brother’s eye, but fail to notice the beam in your own eye?

verses.esv: Or how can you say to your brother, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ when there is the log in your own eye?
verses.kjv: Or how wilt thou say to thy brother, Let me pull out the mote out of thine eye; and, behold, a beam is in thine own eye?
verses.bsb: How can you say to your brother, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ while there is still a beam in your own eye?

verses.esv: You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your brother's eye.
verses.kjv: Thou hypocrite, first cast out the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to cast out the mote out of thy brother’s eye.
verses.bsb: You hypocrite! First take the beam out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.

verses.esv: “Do not give dogs what is holy, and do not throw your pearls before pigs, lest they trample them underfoot and turn to attack you.
verses.kjv: ¶ Give not that which is holy unto the dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn again and rend you.
verses.bsb: Do not give dogs what is holy; do not throw your pearls before swine. If you do, they may trample them under their feet, and then turn and tear you to pieces.

verses.esv: “Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you:
verses.bsb: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened to you.

verses.esv: For everyone who asks receives, and the one who seeks finds, and to the one who knocks it will be opened.
verses.kjv: For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.
verses.bsb: For everyone who asks receives; he who seeks finds; and to him who knocks, the door will be opened.

verses.esv: Or which one of you, if his son asks him for bread, will give him a stone?
verses.kjv: Or what man is there of you, whom if his son ask bread, will he give him a stone?
verses.bsb: Which of you, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?

verses.esv: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a serpent?
verses.kjv: Or if he ask a fish, will he give him a serpent?
verses.bsb: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?

verses.esv: If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
verses.kjv: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?
verses.bsb: So if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask Him!

verses.esv: “So whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets.
verses.kjv: Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them: for this is the law and the prophets.
verses.bsb: In everything, then, do to others as you would have them do to you. For this is the essence of the Law and the Prophets.

verses.esv: “Enter by the narrow gate. For the gate is wide and the way is easy that leads to destruction, and those who enter by it are many.
verses.kjv: ¶ Enter ye in at the strait gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which go in thereat:
verses.bsb: Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many enter through it.

verses.esv: For the gate is narrow and the way is hard that leads to life, and those who find it are few.
verses.kjv: Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it.
verses.bsb: But small is the gate and narrow the way that leads to life, and only a few find it.

verses.esv: “Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep's clothing but inwardly are ravenous wolves.
verses.kjv: ¶ Beware of false prophets, which come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ravening wolves.
verses.bsb: Beware of false prophets. They come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ravenous wolves.

verses.esv: You will recognize them by their fruits. Are grapes gathered from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
verses.kjv: Ye shall know them by their fruits. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?
verses.bsb: By their fruit you will recognize them. Are grapes gathered from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?

verses.esv: So, every healthy tree bears good fruit, but the diseased tree bears bad fruit.
verses.kjv: Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit.
verses.bsb: Likewise, every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit.

verses.esv: A healthy tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a diseased tree bear good fruit.
verses.kjv: A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
verses.bsb: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot bear good fruit.

verses.esv: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
verses.kjv: Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.
verses.bsb: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.

verses.esv: Thus you will recognize them by their fruits.
verses.kjv: Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.
verses.bsb: So then, by their fruit you will recognize them.

verses.esv: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but the one who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
verses.kjv: ¶ Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: Not everyone who says to Me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only he who does the will of My Father in heaven.

verses.esv: On that day many will say to me, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and cast out demons in your name, and do many mighty works in your name?’
verses.kjv: Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works?
verses.bsb: Many will say to Me on that day, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in Your name, and in Your name drive out demons and perform many miracles?’

verses.esv: And then will I declare to them, ‘I never knew you; depart from me, you workers of lawlessness.’
verses.kjv: And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.
verses.bsb: Then I will tell them plainly, ‘I never knew you; depart from Me, you workers of lawlessness!’

verses.esv: “Everyone then who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
verses.kjv: ¶ Therefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, which built his house upon a rock:
verses.bsb: Therefore everyone who hears these words of Mine and acts on them is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.

verses.esv: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house, but it did not fall, because it had been founded on the rock.
verses.kjv: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon a rock.
verses.bsb: The rain fell, the torrents raged, and the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it did not fall, because its foundation was on the rock.

verses.esv: And everyone who hears these words of mine and does not do them will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand.
verses.kjv: And every one that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, which built his house upon the sand:
verses.bsb: But everyone who hears these words of Mine and does not act on them is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.

verses.esv: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell, and great was the fall of it.”
verses.kjv: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell: and great was the fall of it.
verses.bsb: The rain fell, the torrents raged, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell—and great was its collapse!”

verses.esv: And when Jesus finished these sayings, the crowds were astonished at his teaching,
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when Jesus had ended these sayings, the people were astonished at his doctrine:
verses.bsb: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were astonished at His teaching,

verses.esv: for he was teaching them as one who had authority, and not as their scribes.
verses.kjv: For he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
verses.bsb: because He taught as one who had authority, and not as their scribes.

verses.esv: When he came down from the mountain, great crowds followed him.
verses.kjv: WHEN he was come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him.
verses.bsb: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed Him.

verses.esv: And behold, a leper came to him and knelt before him, saying, “Lord, if you will, you can make me clean.”
verses.kjv: And, behold, there came a leper and worshipped him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
verses.bsb: Suddenly a leper came and knelt before Him, saying, “Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.”

verses.esv: And Jesus stretched out his hand and touched him, saying, “I will; be clean.” And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
verses.kjv: And Jesus put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will; be thou clean. And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
verses.bsb: Jesus reached out His hand and touched the man. “I am willing,” He said. “Be clean!” And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.

verses.esv: And Jesus said to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone, but go, show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a proof to them.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, See thou tell no man; but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus instructed him, “See that you don’t tell anyone. But go, show yourself to the priest and offer the gift prescribed by Moses, as a testimony to them.”

verses.esv: When he had entered Capernaum, a centurion came forward to him, appealing to him,
verses.kjv: ¶ And when Jesus was entered into Capernaum, there came unto him a centurion, beseeching him,
verses.bsb: When Jesus had entered Capernaum, a centurion came and pleaded with Him,

verses.esv: “Lord, my servant is lying paralyzed at home, suffering terribly.”
verses.kjv: And saying, Lord, my servant lieth at home sick of the palsy, grievously tormented.
verses.bsb: “Lord, my servant lies at home, paralyzed and in terrible agony.”

verses.esv: And he said to him, “I will come and heal him.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, I will come and heal him.
verses.bsb: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.

verses.esv: But the centurion replied, “Lord, I am not worthy to have you come under my roof, but only say the word, and my servant will be healed.
verses.kjv: The centurion answered and said, Lord, I am not worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof: but speak the word only, and my servant shall be healed.
verses.bsb: The centurion answered, “Lord, I am not worthy to have You come under my roof. But just say the word, and my servant will be healed.

verses.esv: For I too am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. And I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes, and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
verses.kjv: For I am a man under authority, having soldiers under me: and I say to this man, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.
verses.bsb: For I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell one to go, and he goes; and another to come, and he comes. I tell my servant to do something, and he does it.”
