verses.esv: When Jesus heard this, he marveled and said to those who followed him, “Truly, I tell you, with no one in Israel have I found such faith.
verses.kjv: When Jesus heard it, he marvelled, and said to them that followed, Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.
verses.bsb: When Jesus heard this, He marveled and said to those following Him, “Truly I tell you, I have not found anyone in Israel with such great faith.

verses.esv: I tell you, many will come from east and west and recline at table with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven,
verses.kjv: And I say unto you, That many shall come from the east and west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven.
verses.bsb: I say to you that many will come from the east and the west to share the banquet with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.

verses.esv: while the sons of the kingdom will be thrown into the outer darkness. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
verses.kjv: But the children of the kingdom shall be cast out into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
verses.bsb: But the sons of the kingdom will be thrown into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”

verses.esv: And to the centurion Jesus said, “Go; let it be done for you as you have believed.” And the servant was healed at that very moment.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto the centurion, Go thy way; and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee. And his servant was healed in the selfsame hour.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to the centurion, “Go! As you have believed, so will it be done for you.” And his servant was healed at that very hour.

verses.esv: And when Jesus entered Peter's house, he saw his mother-in-law lying sick with a fever.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when Jesus was come into Peter’s house, he saw his wife’s mother laid, and sick of a fever.
verses.bsb: When Jesus arrived at Peter’s house, He saw Peter’s mother-in-law sick in bed with a fever.

verses.esv: He touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she rose and began to serve him.
verses.kjv: And he touched her hand, and the fever left her: and she arose, and ministered unto them.
verses.bsb: So He touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she got up and began to serve Him.

verses.esv: That evening they brought to him many who were oppressed by demons, and he cast out the spirits with a word and healed all who were sick.
verses.kjv: ¶ When the even was come, they brought unto him many that were possessed with devils: and he cast out the spirits with his word, and healed all that were sick:
verses.bsb: When evening came, many who were demon-possessed were brought to Jesus, and He drove out the spirits with a word and healed all the sick.

verses.esv: This was to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah: “He took our illnesses and bore our diseases.”
verses.kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying, Himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses.
verses.bsb: This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:  “He took on our infirmities and carried our diseases.”

verses.esv: Now when Jesus saw a crowd around him, he gave orders to go over to the other side.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now when Jesus saw great multitudes about him, he gave commandment to depart unto the other side.
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw a large crowd around Him, He gave orders to cross to the other side of the sea.

verses.esv: And a scribe came up and said to him, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
verses.kjv: And a certain scribe came, and said unto him, Master, I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest.
verses.bsb: And one of the scribes came to Him and said, “Teacher, I will follow You wherever You go.”

verses.esv: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Foxes have dens and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay His head.”

verses.esv: Another of the disciples said to him, “Lord, let me first go and bury my father.”
verses.kjv: And another of his disciples said unto him, Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.
verses.bsb: Another of His disciples requested, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”

verses.esv: And Jesus said to him, “Follow me, and leave the dead to bury their own dead.”
verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto him, Follow me; and let the dead bury their dead.
verses.bsb: But Jesus told him, “Follow Me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”

verses.esv: And when he got into the boat, his disciples followed him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he was entered into a ship, his disciples followed him.
verses.bsb: When He got into the boat, His disciples followed Him.

verses.esv: And behold, there arose a great storm on the sea, so that the boat was being swamped by the waves; but he was asleep.
verses.kjv: And, behold, there arose a great tempest in the sea, insomuch that the ship was covered with the waves: but he was asleep.
verses.bsb: Suddenly a violent storm came up on the sea, so that the boat was engulfed by the waves; but Jesus was sleeping.

verses.esv: And they went and woke him, saying, “Save us, Lord; we are perishing.”
verses.kjv: And his disciples came to him, and awoke him, saying, Lord, save us: we perish.
verses.bsb: The disciples went and woke Him, saying, “Lord, save us! We are perishing!”

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Why are you afraid, O you of little faith?” Then he rose and rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm.
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Why are ye fearful, O ye of little faith? Then he arose, and rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a great calm.
verses.bsb: “You of little faith,” Jesus replied, “why are you so afraid?” Then He got up and rebuked the winds and the sea, and it was perfectly calm.

verses.esv: And the men marveled, saying, “What sort of man is this, that even winds and sea obey him?”
verses.kjv: But the men marvelled, saying, What manner of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him!
verses.bsb: The men were amazed and asked, “What kind of man is this? Even the winds and the sea obey Him!”

verses.esv: And when he came to the other side, to the country of the Gadarenes, two demon-possessed men met him, coming out of the tombs, so fierce that no one could pass that way.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he was come to the other side into the country of the Gergesenes, there met him two possessed with devils, coming out of the tombs, exceeding fierce, so that no man might pass by that way.
verses.bsb: When Jesus arrived on the other side in the region of the Gadarenes, He was met by two demon-possessed men coming from the tombs. They were so violent that no one could pass that way.

verses.esv: And behold, they cried out, “What have you to do with us, O Son of God? Have you come here to torment us before the time?”
verses.kjv: And, behold, they cried out, saying, What have we to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God? art thou come hither to torment us before the time?
verses.bsb: “What do You want with us, Son of God?” they shouted. “Have You come here to torture us before the appointed time?”

verses.esv: Now a herd of many pigs was feeding at some distance from them.
verses.kjv: And there was a good way off from them an herd of many swine feeding.
verses.bsb: In the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.

verses.esv: And the demons begged him, saying, “If you cast us out, send us away into the herd of pigs.”
verses.kjv: So the devils besought him, saying, If thou cast us out, suffer us to go away into the herd of swine.
verses.bsb: So the demons begged Jesus, “If You drive us out, send us into the herd of pigs.”

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Go.” So they came out and went into the pigs, and behold, the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and drowned in the waters.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Go. And when they were come out, they went into the herd of swine: and, behold, the whole herd of swine ran violently down a steep place into the sea, and perished in the waters.
verses.bsb: “Go!” He told them. So they came out and went into the pigs, and the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and died in the waters.

verses.esv: The herdsmen fled, and going into the city they told everything, especially what had happened to the demon-possessed men.
verses.kjv: And they that kept them fled, and went their ways into the city, and told every thing, and what was befallen to the possessed of the devils.
verses.bsb: Those tending the pigs ran off into the town and reported all this, including the account of the demon-possessed men.

verses.esv: And behold, all the city came out to meet Jesus, and when they saw him, they begged him to leave their region.
verses.kjv: And, behold, the whole city came out to meet Jesus: and when they saw him, they besought him that he would depart out of their coasts.
verses.bsb: Then the whole town went out to meet Jesus. And when they saw Him, they begged Him to leave their region.

verses.esv: And getting into a boat he crossed over and came to his own city.
verses.kjv: AND he entered into a ship, and passed over, and came into his own city.
verses.bsb: Jesus got into a boat, crossed over, and came to His own town.

verses.esv: And behold, some people brought to him a paralytic, lying on a bed. And when Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Take heart, my son; your sins are forgiven.”
verses.kjv: And, behold, they brought to him a man sick of the palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy; Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.
verses.bsb: Just then some men brought to Him a paralytic lying on a mat. When Jesus saw their faith, He said to the paralytic, “Take courage, son; your sins are forgiven.”

verses.esv: And behold, some of the scribes said to themselves, “This man is blaspheming.”
verses.kjv: And, behold, certain of the scribes said within themselves, This man blasphemeth.
verses.bsb: On seeing this, some of the scribes said to themselves, “This man is blaspheming!”

verses.esv: But Jesus, knowing their thoughts, said, “Why do you think evil in your hearts?
verses.kjv: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?
verses.bsb: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, “Why do you harbor evil in your hearts?

verses.esv: For which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Rise and walk’?
verses.kjv: For whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?
verses.bsb: Which is easier: to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk?’

verses.esv: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he then said to the paralytic—“Rise, pick up your bed and go home.”
verses.kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (then saith he to the sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.
verses.bsb: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins...” Then He said to the paralytic, “Get up, pick up your mat, and go home.”

verses.esv: And he rose and went home.
verses.kjv: And he arose, and departed to his house.
verses.bsb: And the man got up and went home.

verses.esv: When the crowds saw it, they were afraid, and they glorified God, who had given such authority to men.
verses.kjv: But when the multitudes saw it, they marvelled, and glorified God, which had given such power unto men.
verses.bsb: When the crowds saw this, they were filled with awe and glorified God, who had given such authority to men.

verses.esv: As Jesus passed on from there, he saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he rose and followed him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Matthew, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.
verses.bsb: As Jesus went on from there, He saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax booth. “Follow Me,” He told him, and Matthew got up and followed Him.

verses.esv: And as Jesus reclined at table in the house, behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and were reclining with Jesus and his disciples.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as Jesus sat at meat in the house, behold, many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.
verses.bsb: Later, as Jesus was dining at Matthew’s house, many tax collectors and sinners came and ate with Him and His disciples.

verses.esv: And when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
verses.kjv: And when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto his disciples, Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?
verses.bsb: When the Pharisees saw this, they asked His disciples, “Why does your Teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”

verses.esv: But when he heard it, he said, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
verses.kjv: But when Jesus heard that, he said unto them, They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, Jesus said, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.

verses.esv: Go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice.’ For I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.”
verses.kjv: But go ye and learn what that meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
verses.bsb: But go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’ For I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.”

verses.esv: Then the disciples of John came to him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?
verses.bsb: At that time John’s disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Why is it that we and the Pharisees fast so often, but Your disciples do not fast?”

verses.esv: And Jesus said to them, “Can the wedding guests mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “How can the guests of the bridegroom mourn while He is with them? But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.

verses.esv: No one puts a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, for the patch tears away from the garment, and a worse tear is made.
verses.kjv: No man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.
verses.bsb: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. For the patch will pull away from the garment, and a worse tear will result.

verses.esv: Neither is new wine put into old wineskins. If it is, the skins burst and the wine is spilled and the skins are destroyed. But new wine is put into fresh wineskins, and so both are preserved.”
verses.kjv: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.
verses.bsb: Neither do men pour new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the skins will burst, the wine will spill, and the wineskins will be ruined. Instead, they pour new wine into new wineskins, and both are preserved.”

verses.esv: While he was saying these things to them, behold, a ruler came in and knelt before him, saying, “My daughter has just died, but come and lay your hand on her, and she will live.”
verses.kjv: ¶ While he spake these things unto them, behold, there came a certain ruler, and worshipped him, saying, My daughter is even now dead: but come and lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.
verses.bsb: While Jesus was saying these things, a synagogue leader came and knelt before Him. “My daughter has just died,” he said. “But come and place Your hand on her, and she will live.”

verses.esv: And Jesus rose and followed him, with his disciples.
verses.kjv: And Jesus arose, and followed him, and so did his disciples.
verses.bsb: So Jesus got up and went with him, along with His disciples.

verses.esv: And behold, a woman who had suffered from a discharge of blood for twelve years came up behind him and touched the fringe of his garment,
verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, a woman, which was diseased with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment:
verses.bsb: Suddenly a woman who had suffered from bleeding for twelve years came up behind Him and touched the fringe of His cloak.

verses.esv: for she said to herself, “If I only touch his garment, I will be made well.”
verses.kjv: For she said within herself, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be whole.
verses.bsb: She said to herself, “If only I touch His cloak, I will be healed.”

verses.esv: Jesus turned, and seeing her he said, “Take heart, daughter; your faith has made you well.” And instantly the woman was made well.
verses.kjv: But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, be of good comfort; thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.
verses.bsb: Jesus turned and saw her. “Take courage, daughter,” He said, “your faith has healed you.” And the woman was cured from that very hour.

verses.esv: And when Jesus came to the ruler's house and saw the flute players and the crowd making a commotion,
verses.kjv: And when Jesus came into the ruler’s house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,
verses.bsb: When Jesus entered the house of the synagogue leader, He saw the flute players and the noisy crowd.

verses.esv: he said, “Go away, for the girl is not dead but sleeping.” And they laughed at him.
verses.kjv: He said unto them, Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.
verses.bsb: “Go away,” He told them. “The girl is not dead, but asleep.” And they laughed at Him.

verses.esv: But when the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took her by the hand, and the girl arose.
verses.kjv: But when the people were put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand, and the maid arose.
verses.bsb: After the crowd had been put outside, Jesus went in and took the girl by the hand, and she got up.

verses.esv: And the report of this went through all that district.
verses.kjv: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.
verses.bsb: And the news about this spread throughout that region.

verses.esv: And as Jesus passed on from there, two blind men followed him, crying aloud, “Have mercy on us, Son of David.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed him, crying, and saying, Thou Son of David, have mercy on us.
verses.bsb: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed Him, crying out, “Have mercy on us, Son of David!”

verses.esv: When he entered the house, the blind men came to him, and Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” They said to him, “Yes, Lord.”
verses.kjv: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had entered the house, the blind men came to Him. “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” He asked.  “Yes, Lord,” they answered.

verses.esv: Then he touched their eyes, saying, “According to your faith be it done to you.”
verses.kjv: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith be it unto you.
verses.bsb: Then He touched their eyes and said, “According to your faith will it be done to you.”

verses.esv: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus sternly warned them, “See that no one knows about it.”
verses.kjv: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus straitly charged them, saying, See that no man know it.
verses.bsb: And their eyes were opened. Jesus warned them sternly, “See that no one finds out about this!”

verses.esv: But they went away and spread his fame through all that district.
verses.kjv: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad his fame in all that country.
verses.bsb: But they went out and spread the news about Him throughout the land.

verses.esv: As they were going away, behold, a demon-oppressed man who was mute was brought to him.
verses.kjv: ¶ As they went out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a devil.
verses.bsb: As they were leaving, a demon-possessed man who was mute was brought to Jesus.

verses.esv: And when the demon had been cast out, the mute man spoke. And the crowds marveled, saying, “Never was anything like this seen in Israel.”
verses.kjv: And when the devil was cast out, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.
verses.bsb: And when the demon had been driven out, the man began to speak. The crowds were amazed and said, “Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel!”

verses.esv: But the Pharisees said, “He casts out demons by the prince of demons.”
verses.kjv: But the Pharisees said, He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.
verses.bsb: But the Pharisees said, “It is by the prince of demons that He drives out demons.”

verses.esv: And Jesus went throughout all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom and healing every disease and every affliction.
verses.kjv: And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.
verses.bsb: Jesus went through all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness.

verses.esv: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
verses.kjv: ¶ But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.
verses.bsb: When He saw the crowds, He was moved with compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.

verses.esv: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few;
verses.kjv: Then saith he unto his disciples, The harvest truly is plenteous, but the labourers are few;
verses.bsb: Then He said to His disciples, “The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few.

verses.esv: therefore pray earnestly to the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.”
verses.kjv: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.
verses.bsb: Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into His harvest.”

verses.esv: And he called to him his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal every disease and every affliction.
verses.kjv: AND when he had called unto him his twelve disciples, he gave them power against unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal all manner of sickness and all manner of disease.
verses.bsb: And calling His twelve disciples to Him, Jesus gave them authority over unclean spirits, so that they could drive them out and heal every disease and sickness.

verses.esv: The names of the twelve apostles are these: first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother;
verses.kjv: Now the names of the twelve apostles are these; The first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother;
verses.bsb: These are the names of the twelve apostles: first Simon, called Peter, and his brother Andrew; James son of Zebedee, and his brother John;

verses.esv: Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus;
verses.kjv: Philip, and Bartholomew; Thomas, and Matthew the publican; James the son of Alphæus, and Lebbæus, whose surname was Thaddæus;
verses.bsb: Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus;

verses.esv: Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
verses.kjv: Simon the Canaanite, and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.
verses.bsb: Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.

verses.esv: These twelve Jesus sent out, instructing them, “Go nowhere among the Gentiles and enter no town of the Samaritans,
verses.kjv: These twelve Jesus sent forth, and commanded them, saying, Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not:
verses.bsb: These twelve Jesus sent out with the following instructions: “Do not go onto the road of the Gentiles or enter any town of the Samaritans.

verses.esv: but go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
verses.kjv: But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
verses.bsb: Go rather to the lost sheep of Israel.

verses.esv: And proclaim as you go, saying, ‘The kingdom of heaven is at hand.’
verses.kjv: And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven is at hand.
verses.bsb: As you go, preach this message: ‘The kingdom of heaven is near.’

verses.esv: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse lepers, cast out demons. You received without paying; give without pay.
verses.kjv: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out devils: freely ye have received, freely give.
verses.bsb: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, drive out demons. Freely you have received; freely give.

verses.esv: Acquire no gold or silver or copper for your belts,
verses.kjv: Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses,
verses.bsb: Do not carry any gold or silver or copper in your belts.

verses.esv: no bag for your journey, or two tunics or sandals or a staff, for the laborer deserves his food.
verses.kjv: Nor scrip for your journey, neither two coats, neither shoes, nor yet staves: for the workman is worthy of his meat.
verses.bsb: Take no bag for the road, or second tunic, or sandals, or staff; for the worker is worthy of his provisions.

verses.esv: And whatever town or village you enter, find out who is worthy in it and stay there until you depart.
verses.kjv: And into whatsoever city or town ye shall enter, inquire who in it is worthy; and there abide till ye go thence.
verses.bsb: Whatever town or village you enter, find out who is worthy there and stay at his house until you move on.

verses.esv: As you enter the house, greet it.
verses.kjv: And when ye come into an house, salute it.
verses.bsb: As you enter the home, greet its occupants.

verses.esv: And if the house is worthy, let your peace come upon it, but if it is not worthy, let your peace return to you.
verses.kjv: And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.
verses.bsb: If the home is worthy, let your peace rest on it; but if it is not, let your peace return to you.

verses.esv: And if anyone will not receive you or listen to your words, shake off the dust from your feet when you leave that house or town.
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.
verses.bsb: And if anyone will not welcome you or heed your words, shake the dust off your feet when you leave that home or town.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, it will be more bearable on the day of judgment for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, it will be more bearable for Sodom and Gomorrah on the day of judgment than for that town.

verses.esv: “Behold, I am sending you out as sheep in the midst of wolves, so be wise as serpents and innocent as doves.
verses.kjv: ¶ Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.
verses.bsb: Behold, I am sending you out like sheep among wolves; therefore be as shrewd as snakes and as innocent as doves.

verses.esv: Beware of men, for they will deliver you over to courts and flog you in their synagogues,
verses.kjv: But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to the councils, and they will scourge you in their synagogues;
verses.bsb: But beware of men; for they will hand you over to their councils and flog you in their synagogues.

verses.esv: and you will be dragged before governors and kings for my sake, to bear witness before them and the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: And ye shall be brought before governors and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them and the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: On My account, you will be brought before governors and kings as witnesses to them and to the Gentiles.

verses.esv: When they deliver you over, do not be anxious how you are to speak or what you are to say, for what you are to say will be given to you in that hour.
verses.kjv: But when they deliver you up, take no thought how or what ye shall speak: for it shall be given you in that same hour what ye shall speak.
verses.bsb: But when they hand you over, do not worry about how to respond or what to say. In that hour you will be given what to say.

verses.esv: For it is not you who speak, but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.
verses.kjv: For it is not ye that speak, but the Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you.
verses.bsb: For it will not be you speaking, but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.

verses.esv: Brother will deliver brother over to death, and the father his child, and children will rise against parents and have them put to death,
verses.kjv: And the brother shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the child: and the children shall rise up against their parents, and cause them to be put to death.
verses.bsb: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child; children will rise against their parents and have them put to death.

verses.esv: and you will be hated by all for my name's sake. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
verses.kjv: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that endureth to the end shall be saved.
verses.bsb: You will be hated by everyone because of My name, but the one who perseveres to the end will be saved.

verses.esv: When they persecute you in one town, flee to the next, for truly, I say to you, you will not have gone through all the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.
verses.kjv: But when they persecute you in this city, flee ye into another: for verily I say unto you, Ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be come.
verses.bsb: When they persecute you in one town, flee to the next. Truly I tell you, you will not reach all the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.

verses.esv: “A disciple is not above his teacher, nor a servant above his master.
verses.kjv: The disciple is not above his master, nor the servant above his lord.
verses.bsb: A disciple is not above his teacher, nor a servant above his master.

verses.esv: It is enough for the disciple to be like his teacher, and the servant like his master. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebul, how much more will they malign those of his household.
verses.kjv: It is enough for the disciple that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more shall they call them of his household?
verses.bsb: It is enough for a disciple to be like his teacher, and a servant like his master. If the head of the house has been called Beelzebul, how much more the members of his household!

verses.esv: “So have no fear of them, for nothing is covered that will not be revealed, or hidden that will not be known.
verses.kjv: Fear them not therefore: for there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known.
verses.bsb: So do not be afraid of them. For there is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed, and nothing hidden that will not be made known.

verses.esv: What I tell you in the dark, say in the light, and what you hear whispered, proclaim on the housetops.
verses.kjv: What I tell you in darkness, that speak ye in light: and what ye hear in the ear, that preach ye upon the housetops.
verses.bsb: What I tell you in the dark, speak in the daylight; what is whispered in your ear, proclaim from the housetops.

verses.esv: And do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Rather fear him who can destroy both soul and body in hell.
verses.kjv: And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.
verses.bsb: Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Instead, fear the One who can destroy both soul and body in hell.

verses.esv: Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? And not one of them will fall to the ground apart from your Father.
verses.kjv: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.
verses.bsb: Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? Yet not one of them will fall to the ground apart from the will of your Father.

verses.esv: But even the hairs of your head are all numbered.
verses.kjv: But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.
verses.bsb: And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered.

verses.esv: Fear not, therefore; you are of more value than many sparrows.
verses.kjv: Fear ye not therefore, ye are of more value than many sparrows.
verses.bsb: So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.

verses.esv: So everyone who acknowledges me before men, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven,
verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore shall confess me before men, him will I confess also before my Father which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: Therefore everyone who confesses Me before men, I will also confess him before My Father in heaven.

verses.esv: but whoever denies me before men, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
verses.kjv: But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: But whoever denies Me before men, I will also deny him before My Father in heaven.

verses.esv: “Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the earth. I have not come to bring peace, but a sword.
verses.kjv: Think not that I am come to send peace on earth: I came not to send peace, but a sword.
verses.bsb: Do not assume that I have come to bring peace to the earth; I have not come to bring peace, but a sword.

verses.esv: For I have come to set a man against his father, and a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.
verses.kjv: For I am come to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.
verses.bsb: For I have come to turn  ‘a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.

verses.esv: And a person's enemies will be those of his own household.
verses.kjv: And a man’s foes shall be they of his own household.
verses.bsb: A man’s enemies will be the members of his own household.’

verses.esv: Whoever loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me, and whoever loves son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
verses.kjv: He that loveth father or mother more than me is not worthy of me: and he that loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
verses.bsb: Anyone who loves his father or mother more than Me is not worthy of Me; anyone who loves his son or daughter more than Me is not worthy of Me;

verses.esv: And whoever does not take his cross and follow me is not worthy of me.
verses.kjv: And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.
verses.bsb: and anyone who does not take up his cross and follow Me is not worthy of Me.

verses.esv: Whoever finds his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life for my sake will find it.
verses.kjv: He that findeth his life shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it.
verses.bsb: Whoever finds his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life for My sake will find it.

verses.esv: “Whoever receives you receives me, and whoever receives me receives him who sent me.
verses.kjv: ¶ He that receiveth you receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
verses.bsb: He who receives you receives Me, and he who receives Me receives the One who sent Me.

verses.esv: The one who receives a prophet because he is a prophet will receive a prophet's reward, and the one who receives a righteous person because he is a righteous person will receive a righteous person's reward.
verses.kjv: He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet’s reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man’s reward.
verses.bsb: Whoever receives a prophet because he is a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward, and whoever receives a righteous man because he is a righteous man will receive a righteous man’s reward.

verses.esv: And whoever gives one of these little ones even a cup of cold water because he is a disciple, truly, I say to you, he will by no means lose his reward.”
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.
verses.bsb: And if anyone gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones because he is My disciple, truly I tell you, he will never lose his reward.”

verses.esv: When Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, when Jesus had made an end of commanding his twelve disciples, he departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had finished instructing His twelve disciples, He went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.

verses.esv: Now when John heard in prison about the deeds of the Christ, he sent word by his disciples
verses.kjv: Now when John had heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples,
verses.bsb: Meanwhile John heard in prison about the works of Christ, and he sent his disciples

verses.esv: and said to him, “Are you the one who is to come, or shall we look for another?”
verses.kjv: And said unto him, Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?
verses.bsb: to ask Him, “Are You the One who was to come, or should we look for someone else?”

verses.esv: And Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John what you hear and see:
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see:
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Go back and report to John what you hear and see:

verses.esv: the blind receive their sight and the lame walk, lepers are cleansed and the deaf hear, and the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.
verses.kjv: The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.
verses.bsb: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is preached to the poor.

verses.esv: And blessed is the one who is not offended by me.”
verses.kjv: And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the one who does not fall away on account of Me.”

verses.esv: As they went away, Jesus began to speak to the crowds concerning John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
verses.kjv: ¶ And as they departed, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken with the wind?
verses.bsb: As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swaying in the wind?

verses.esv: What then did you go out to see? A man dressed in soft clothing? Behold, those who wear soft clothing are in kings' houses.
verses.kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? behold, they that wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.
verses.bsb: Otherwise, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? Look, those who wear fine clothing are found in kings’ palaces.

verses.esv: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
verses.kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? yea, I say unto you, and more than a prophet.
verses.bsb: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.

verses.esv: This is he of whom it is written, “‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you.’
verses.kjv: For this is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
verses.bsb: This is the one about whom it is written:  ‘Behold, I will send My messenger ahead of You, who will prepare Your way before You.’

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, among those born of women there has arisen no one greater than John the Baptist. Yet the one who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Among them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding he that is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, among those born of women there has risen no one greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.

verses.esv: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven has suffered violence, and the violent take it by force.
verses.kjv: And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.
verses.bsb: From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been subject to violence, and the violent lay claim to it.

verses.esv: For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John,
verses.kjv: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
verses.bsb: For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.

verses.esv: and if you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
verses.kjv: And if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come.
verses.bsb: And if you are willing to accept it, he is the Elijah who was to come.

verses.esv: He who has ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.kjv: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.bsb: He who has ears, let him hear.

verses.esv: “But to what shall I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling to their playmates,
verses.kjv: ¶ But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows,
verses.bsb: To what can I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling out to others:

verses.esv: “‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not mourn.’
verses.kjv: And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented.
verses.bsb: ‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not mourn.’

verses.esv: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon.’
verses.kjv: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil.
verses.bsb: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon!’

verses.esv: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look at him! A glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ Yet wisdom is justified by her deeds.”
verses.kjv: The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified of her children.
verses.bsb: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look at this glutton and drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is vindicated by her actions.”

verses.esv: Then he began to denounce the cities where most of his mighty works had been done, because they did not repent.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then began he to upbraid the cities wherein most of his mighty works were done, because they repented not:
verses.bsb: Then Jesus began to denounce the cities in which most of His miracles had been performed, because they did not repent.

verses.esv: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
verses.kjv: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
verses.bsb: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.

verses.esv: But I tell you, it will be more bearable on the day of judgment for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the day of judgment, than for you.
verses.bsb: But I tell you, it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.

verses.esv: And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven? You will be brought down to Hades. For if the mighty works done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
verses.kjv: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted unto heaven, shalt be brought down to hell: for if the mighty works, which have been done in thee, had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
verses.bsb: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.

verses.esv: But I tell you that it will be more tolerable on the day of judgment for the land of Sodom than for you.”
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment, than for thee.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that it will be more bearable for Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.”

verses.esv: At that time Jesus declared, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children;
verses.kjv: ¶ At that time Jesus answered and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.
verses.bsb: At that time Jesus declared, “I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children.

verses.esv: yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.
verses.kjv: Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.
verses.bsb: Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.

verses.esv: All things have been handed over to me by my Father, and no one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
verses.kjv: All things are delivered unto me of my Father: and no man knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither knoweth any man the Father, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal him.
verses.bsb: All things have been entrusted to Me by My Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.

verses.esv: Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
verses.kjv: ¶ Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
verses.bsb: Come to Me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.

verses.esv: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
verses.kjv: Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.
verses.bsb: Take My yoke upon you and learn from Me; for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.

verses.esv: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”
verses.kjv: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
verses.bsb: For My yoke is easy and My burden is light.”

verses.esv: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry, and they began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.
verses.kjv: AT that time Jesus went on the sabbath day through the corn; and his disciples were an hungred, and began to pluck the ears of corn, and to eat.
verses.bsb: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry and began to pick the heads of grain and eat them.

verses.esv: But when the Pharisees saw it, they said to him, “Look, your disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath.”
verses.kjv: But when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto him, Behold, thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to Him, “Look, Your disciples are doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath.”

verses.esv: He said to them, “Have you not read what David did when he was hungry, and those who were with him:
verses.kjv: But he said unto them, Have ye not read what David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him;
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?

verses.esv: how he entered the house of God and ate the bread of the Presence, which it was not lawful for him to eat nor for those who were with him, but only for the priests?
verses.kjv: How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests?
verses.bsb: He entered the house of God, and he and his companions ate the consecrated bread, which was not lawful for them to eat, but only for the priests.

verses.esv: Or have you not read in the Law how on the Sabbath the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath and are guiltless?
verses.kjv: Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless?
verses.bsb: Or haven’t you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple break the Sabbath and yet are innocent?

verses.esv: I tell you, something greater than the temple is here.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That in this place is one greater than the temple.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.

verses.esv: And if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the guiltless.
verses.kjv: But if ye had known what this meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.
verses.bsb: If only you had known the meaning of ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the innocent.

verses.esv: For the Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.”
verses.kjv: For the Son of man is Lord even of the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”

verses.esv: He went on from there and entered their synagogue.
verses.kjv: And when he was departed thence, he went into their synagogue:
verses.bsb: Moving on from there, Jesus entered their synagogue,

verses.esv: And a man was there with a withered hand. And they asked him, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”—so that they might accuse him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, there was a man which had his hand withered. And they asked him, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath days? that they might accuse him.
verses.bsb: and a man with a withered hand was there. In order to accuse Jesus, they asked Him, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”

verses.esv: He said to them, “Which one of you who has a sheep, if it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will not take hold of it and lift it out?
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What man shall there be among you, that shall have one sheep, and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift it out?
verses.bsb: He replied, “If one of you has a sheep and it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will he not take hold of it and lift it out?

verses.esv: Of how much more value is a man than a sheep! So it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.”
verses.kjv: How much then is a man better than a sheep? Wherefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days.
verses.bsb: How much more valuable is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.”

verses.esv: Then he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” And the man stretched it out, and it was restored, healthy like the other.
verses.kjv: Then saith he to the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it forth; and it was restored whole, like as the other.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” So he stretched it out, and it was restored to full use, just like the other.

verses.esv: But the Pharisees went out and conspired against him, how to destroy him.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then the Pharisees went out, and held a council against him, how they might destroy him.
verses.bsb: But the Pharisees went out and plotted how they might kill Jesus.

verses.esv: Jesus, aware of this, withdrew from there. And many followed him, and he healed them all
verses.kjv: But when Jesus knew it, he withdrew himself from thence: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them all;
verses.bsb: Aware of this, Jesus withdrew from that place. Large crowds followed Him, and He healed them all,

verses.esv: and ordered them not to make him known.
verses.kjv: And charged them that they should not make him known:
verses.bsb: warning them not to make Him known.

verses.esv: This was to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah:
verses.kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying,
verses.bsb: This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:

verses.esv: “Behold, my servant whom I have chosen, my beloved with whom my soul is well pleased. I will put my Spirit upon him, and he will proclaim justice to the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: Behold my servant, whom I have chosen; my beloved, in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my spirit upon him, and he shall shew judgment to the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: “Here is My Servant, whom I have chosen, My beloved, in whom My soul delights. I will put My Spirit on Him, and He will proclaim justice to the nations.

verses.esv: He will not quarrel or cry aloud, nor will anyone hear his voice in the streets;
verses.kjv: He shall not strive, nor cry; neither shall any man hear his voice in the streets.
verses.bsb: He will not quarrel or cry out; no one will hear His voice in the streets.

verses.esv: a bruised reed he will not break, and a smoldering wick he will not quench, until he brings justice to victory;
verses.kjv: A bruised reed shall he not break, and smoking flax shall he not quench, till he send forth judgment unto victory.
verses.bsb: A bruised reed He will not break, and a smoldering wick He will not extinguish, till He leads justice to victory.

verses.esv: and in his name the Gentiles will hope.”
verses.kjv: And in his name shall the Gentiles trust.
verses.bsb: In His name the nations will put their hope.”

verses.esv: Then a demon-oppressed man who was blind and mute was brought to him, and he healed him, so that the man spoke and saw.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then was brought unto him one possessed with a devil, blind, and dumb: and he healed him, insomuch that the blind and dumb both spake and saw.
verses.bsb: Then a demon-possessed man who was blind and mute was brought to Jesus, and He healed the man so that he could speak and see.

verses.esv: And all the people were amazed, and said, “Can this be the Son of David?”
verses.kjv: And all the people were amazed, and said, Is not this the son of David?
verses.bsb: The crowds were astounded and asked, “Could this be the Son of David?”

verses.esv: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, “It is only by Beelzebul, the prince of demons, that this man casts out demons.”
verses.kjv: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils, but by Beelzebub the prince of the devils.
verses.bsb: But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, “Only by Beelzebul, the prince of demons, does this man drive out demons.”

verses.esv: Knowing their thoughts, he said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste, and no city or house divided against itself will stand.
verses.kjv: And Jesus knew their thoughts, and said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand:
verses.bsb: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste, and every city or household divided against itself will not stand.

verses.esv: And if Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?
verses.kjv: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand?
verses.bsb: If Satan drives out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then can his kingdom stand?

verses.esv: And if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
verses.kjv: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out? therefore they shall be your judges.
verses.bsb: And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons drive them out? So then, they will be your judges.

verses.esv: But if it is by the Spirit of God that I cast out demons, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.
verses.kjv: But if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God is come unto you.
verses.bsb: But if I drive out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.

verses.esv: Or how can someone enter a strong man's house and plunder his goods, unless he first binds the strong man? Then indeed he may plunder his house.
verses.kjv: Or else how can one enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house.
verses.bsb: Or again, how can anyone enter a strong man’s house and steal his possessions, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can plunder his house.

verses.esv: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.
verses.kjv: He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.
verses.bsb: He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters.

verses.esv: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven people, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
verses.kjv: ¶ Wherefore I say unto you, All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men: but the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost shall not be forgiven unto men.
verses.bsb: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.

verses.esv: And whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the age to come.
verses.kjv: And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.
verses.bsb: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the one to come.

verses.esv: “Either make the tree good and its fruit good, or make the tree bad and its fruit bad, for the tree is known by its fruit.
verses.kjv: Either make the tree good, and his fruit good; or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by his fruit.
verses.bsb: Make a tree good and its fruit will be good, or make a tree bad and its fruit will be bad; for a tree is known by its fruit.

verses.esv: You brood of vipers! How can you speak good, when you are evil? For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.
verses.kjv: O generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.
verses.bsb: You brood of vipers, how can you who are evil say anything good? For out of the overflow of the heart, the mouth speaks.

verses.esv: The good person out of his good treasure brings forth good, and the evil person out of his evil treasure brings forth evil.
verses.kjv: A good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.
verses.bsb: The good man brings good things out of his good store of treasure, and the evil man brings evil things out of his evil store of treasure.

verses.esv: I tell you, on the day of judgment people will give account for every careless word they speak,
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that men will give an account on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken.

verses.esv: for by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”
verses.kjv: For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.
verses.bsb: For by your words you will be acquitted, and by your words you will be condemned.”

verses.esv: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees answered him, saying, “Teacher, we wish to see a sign from you.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then certain of the scribes and of the Pharisees answered, saying, Master, we would see a sign from thee.
verses.bsb: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to Him, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from You.”

verses.esv: But he answered them, “An evil and adulterous generation seeks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
verses.kjv: But he answered and said unto them, An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “A wicked and adulterous generation demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.

verses.esv: For just as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
verses.kjv: For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale’s belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
verses.bsb: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so the Son of Man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

verses.esv: The men of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and behold, something greater than Jonah is here.
verses.kjv: The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.
verses.bsb: The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now One greater than Jonah is here.

verses.esv: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and behold, something greater than Solomon is here.
verses.kjv: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.
verses.bsb: The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and now One greater than Solomon is here.

verses.esv: “When the unclean spirit has gone out of a person, it passes through waterless places seeking rest, but finds none.
verses.kjv: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.
verses.bsb: When an unclean spirit comes out of a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it.

verses.esv: Then it says, ‘I will return to my house from which I came.’ And when it comes, it finds the house empty, swept, and put in order.
verses.kjv: Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.
verses.bsb: Then it says, ‘I will return to the house I left.’ On its return, it finds the house vacant, swept clean, and put in order.

verses.esv: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they enter and dwell there, and the last state of that person is worse than the first. So also will it be with this evil generation.”
verses.kjv: Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation.
verses.bsb: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and dwell there; and the final plight of that man is worse than the first. So will it be with this wicked generation.”

verses.esv: While he was still speaking to the people, behold, his mother and his brothers stood outside, asking to speak to him.
verses.kjv: ¶ While he yet talked to the people, behold, his mother and his brethren stood without, desiring to speak with him.
verses.bsb: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, His mother and brothers stood outside, wanting to speak to Him.

verses.esv: But he replied to the man who told him, “Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?”
verses.kjv: But he answered and said unto him that told him, Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “Who is My mother, and who are My brothers?”

verses.esv: And stretching out his hand toward his disciples, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
verses.kjv: And he stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
verses.bsb: Pointing to His disciples, He said, “Here are My mother and My brothers.

verses.esv: For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.”
verses.kjv: For whosoever shall do the will of my Father which is in heaven, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.
verses.bsb: For whoever does the will of My Father in heaven is My brother and sister and mother.”

verses.esv: That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat beside the sea.
verses.kjv: THE same day went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the sea side.
verses.bsb: That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the sea.

verses.esv: And great crowds gathered about him, so that he got into a boat and sat down. And the whole crowd stood on the beach.
verses.kjv: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went into a ship, and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
verses.bsb: Such large crowds gathered around Him that He got into a boat and sat down, while all the people stood on the shore.

verses.esv: And he told them many things in parables, saying: “A sower went out to sow.
verses.kjv: And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went forth to sow;
verses.bsb: And He told them many things in parables, saying, “A farmer went out to sow his seed.

verses.esv: And as he sowed, some seeds fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured them.
verses.kjv: And when he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:
verses.bsb: And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.

verses.esv: Other seeds fell on rocky ground, where they did not have much soil, and immediately they sprang up, since they had no depth of soil,
verses.kjv: Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:
verses.bsb: Some fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow.

verses.esv: but when the sun rose they were scorched. And since they had no root, they withered away.
verses.kjv: And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.
verses.bsb: But when the sun rose, the seedlings were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.

verses.esv: Other seeds fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked them.
verses.kjv: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:
verses.bsb: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the seedlings.

verses.esv: Other seeds fell on good soil and produced grain, some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
verses.kjv: But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.
verses.bsb: Still other seed fell on good soil and produced a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.

verses.esv: He who has ears, let him hear.”
verses.kjv: Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.bsb: He who has ears, let him hear.”

verses.esv: Then the disciples came and said to him, “Why do you speak to them in parables?”
verses.kjv: And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in parables?
verses.bsb: Then the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Why do You speak to the people in parables?”

verses.esv: And he answered them, “To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been given.
verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.
verses.bsb: He replied, “The knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven has been given to you, but not to them.

verses.esv: For to the one who has, more will be given, and he will have an abundance, but from the one who has not, even what he has will be taken away.
verses.kjv: For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.
verses.bsb: Whoever has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.

verses.esv: This is why I speak to them in parables, because seeing they do not see, and hearing they do not hear, nor do they understand.
verses.kjv: Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.
verses.bsb: This is why I speak to them in parables:  ‘Though seeing, they do not see; though hearing, they do not hear or understand.’

verses.esv: Indeed, in their case the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled that says: “‘“You will indeed hear but never understand, and you will indeed see but never perceive.”
verses.kjv: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:
verses.bsb: In them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled:  ‘You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving.

verses.esv: For this people's heart has grown dull, and with their ears they can barely hear, and their eyes they have closed, lest they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears and understand with their heart and turn, and I would heal them.’
verses.kjv: For this people’s heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.
verses.bsb: For this people’s heart has grown callous; they hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, and turn, and I would heal them.’

verses.esv: But blessed are your eyes, for they see, and your ears, for they hear.
verses.kjv: But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.
verses.bsb: But blessed are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear.

verses.esv: For truly, I say to you, many prophets and righteous people longed to see what you see, and did not see it, and to hear what you hear, and did not hear it.
verses.kjv: For verily I say unto you, That many prophets and righteous men have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.
verses.bsb: For truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous men longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.

verses.esv: “Hear then the parable of the sower:
verses.kjv: ¶ Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.
verses.bsb: Consider, then, the parable of the sower:

verses.esv: When anyone hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what has been sown in his heart. This is what was sown along the path.
verses.kjv: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.
verses.bsb: When anyone hears the message of the kingdom but does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. This is the seed sown along the path.

verses.esv: As for what was sown on rocky ground, this is the one who hears the word and immediately receives it with joy,
verses.kjv: But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;
verses.bsb: The seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.

verses.esv: yet he has no root in himself, but endures for a while, and when tribulation or persecution arises on account of the word, immediately he falls away.
verses.kjv: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.
verses.bsb: But since he has no root, he remains for only a season. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, he quickly falls away.

verses.esv: As for what was sown among thorns, this is the one who hears the word, but the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of riches choke the word, and it proves unfruitful.
verses.kjv: He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.
verses.bsb: The seed sown among the thorns is the one who hears the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

verses.esv: As for what was sown on good soil, this is the one who hears the word and understands it. He indeed bears fruit and yields, in one case a hundredfold, in another sixty, and in another thirty.”
verses.kjv: But he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word, and understandeth it; which also beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
verses.bsb: But the seed sown on good soil is the one who hears the word and understands it. He indeed bears fruit and produces a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.”

verses.esv: He put another parable before them, saying, “The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a man who sowed good seed in his field,
verses.kjv: ¶ Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field:
verses.bsb: Jesus put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field.

verses.esv: but while his men were sleeping, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat and went away.
verses.kjv: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.
verses.bsb: But while everyone was asleep, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and slipped away.

verses.esv: So when the plants came up and bore grain, then the weeds appeared also.
verses.kjv: But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.
verses.bsb: When the wheat sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.

verses.esv: And the servants of the master of the house came and said to him, ‘Master, did you not sow good seed in your field? How then does it have weeds?’
verses.kjv: So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?
verses.bsb: The owner’s servants came to him and said, ‘Sir, didn’t you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?’

verses.esv: He said to them, ‘An enemy has done this.’ So the servants said to him, ‘Then do you want us to go and gather them?’
verses.kjv: He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?
verses.bsb: ‘An enemy did this,’ he replied.  So the servants asked him, ‘Do you want us to go and pull them up?’

verses.esv: But he said, ‘No, lest in gathering the weeds you root up the wheat along with them.
verses.kjv: But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.
