verses.esv: Honor your father and mother, and, You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
verses.kjv: Honour thy father and thy mother: and, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
verses.bsb: honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as yourself.’”

verses.esv: The young man said to him, “All these I have kept. What do I still lack?”
verses.kjv: The young man saith unto him, All these things have I kept from my youth up: what lack I yet?
verses.bsb: “All these I have kept,” said the young man. “What do I still lack?”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “If you would be perfect, go, sell what you possess and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.”
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto him, If thou wilt be perfect, go and sell that thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come and follow me.
verses.bsb: Jesus told him, “If you want to be perfect, go, sell your possessions and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow Me.”

verses.esv: When the young man heard this he went away sorrowful, for he had great possessions.
verses.kjv: But when the young man heard that saying, he went away sorrowful: for he had great possessions.
verses.bsb: When the young man heard this, he went away in sorrow, because he had great wealth.

verses.esv: And Jesus said to his disciples, “Truly, I say to you, only with difficulty will a rich person enter the kingdom of heaven.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then said Jesus unto his disciples, Verily I say unto you, That a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to His disciples, “Truly I tell you, it is hard for a rich man to enter the kingdom of heaven.

verses.esv: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.”
verses.kjv: And again I say unto you, It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.”

verses.esv: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astonished, saying, “Who then can be saved?”
verses.kjv: When his disciples heard it, they were exceedingly amazed, saying, Who then can be saved?
verses.bsb: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astonished and asked, “Who then can be saved?”

verses.esv: But Jesus looked at them and said, “With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”
verses.kjv: But Jesus beheld them, and said unto them, With men this is impossible; but with God all things are possible.
verses.bsb: Jesus looked at them and said, “With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”

verses.esv: Then Peter said in reply, “See, we have left everything and followed you. What then will we have?”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then answered Peter and said unto him, Behold, we have forsaken all, and followed thee; what shall we have therefore?
verses.bsb: “Look,” Peter replied, “we have left everything to follow You. What then will there be for us?”

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “Truly, I say to you, in the new world, when the Son of Man will sit on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, in the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man sits on His glorious throne, you who have followed Me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

verses.esv: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or lands, for my name's sake, will receive a hundredfold and will inherit eternal life.
verses.kjv: And every one that hath forsaken houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my name’s sake, shall receive an hundredfold, and shall inherit everlasting life.
verses.bsb: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or wife or children or fields for the sake of My name will receive a hundredfold and will inherit eternal life.

verses.esv: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.
verses.kjv: But many that are first shall be last; and the last shall be first.
verses.bsb: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.

verses.esv: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a master of a house who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
verses.kjv: FOR the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which went out early in the morning to hire labourers into his vineyard.
verses.bsb: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.

verses.esv: After agreeing with the laborers for a denarius a day, he sent them into his vineyard.
verses.kjv: And when he had agreed with the labourers for a penny a day, he sent them into his vineyard.
verses.bsb: He agreed to pay them a denarius for the day and sent them into his vineyard.

verses.esv: And going out about the third hour he saw others standing idle in the marketplace,
verses.kjv: And he went out about the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the marketplace,
verses.bsb: About the third hour he went out and saw others standing in the marketplace doing nothing.

verses.esv: and to them he said, ‘You go into the vineyard too, and whatever is right I will give you.’
verses.kjv: And said unto them; Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you. And they went their way.
verses.bsb: ‘You also go into my vineyard,’ he said, ‘and I will pay you whatever is right.’

verses.esv: So they went. Going out again about the sixth hour and the ninth hour, he did the same.
verses.kjv: Again he went out about the sixth and ninth hour, and did likewise.
verses.bsb: So they went.  He went out again about the sixth hour and the ninth hour and did the same thing.

verses.esv: And about the eleventh hour he went out and found others standing. And he said to them, ‘Why do you stand here idle all day?’
verses.kjv: And about the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle, and saith unto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?
verses.bsb: About the eleventh hour he went out and found still others standing around. ‘Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?’ he asked.

verses.esv: They said to him, ‘Because no one has hired us.’ He said to them, ‘You go into the vineyard too.’
verses.kjv: They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard; and whatsoever is right, that shall ye receive.
verses.bsb: ‘Because no one has hired us,’ they answered.  So he told them, ‘You also go into my vineyard.’

verses.esv: And when evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman, ‘Call the laborers and pay them their wages, beginning with the last, up to the first.’
verses.kjv: So when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the labourers, and give them their hire, beginning from the last unto the first.
verses.bsb: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman, ‘Call the workers and pay them their wages, starting with the last ones hired and moving on to the first.’

verses.esv: And when those hired about the eleventh hour came, each of them received a denarius.
verses.kjv: And when they came that were hired about the eleventh hour, they received every man a penny.
verses.bsb: The workers who were hired about the eleventh hour came and each received a denarius.

verses.esv: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more, but each of them also received a denarius.
verses.kjv: But when the first came, they supposed that they should have received more; and they likewise received every man a penny.
verses.bsb: So when the original workers came, they assumed they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarius.

verses.esv: And on receiving it they grumbled at the master of the house,
verses.kjv: And when they had received it, they murmured against the goodman of the house,
verses.bsb: On receiving their pay, they began to grumble against the landowner.

verses.esv: saying, ‘These last worked only one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat.’
verses.kjv: Saying, These last have wrought but one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us, which have borne the burden and heat of the day.
verses.bsb: ‘These men who were hired last worked only one hour,’ they said, ‘and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden and the scorching heat of the day.’

verses.esv: But he replied to one of them, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong. Did you not agree with me for a denarius?
verses.kjv: But he answered one of them, and said, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst not thou agree with me for a penny?
verses.bsb: But he answered one of them, ‘Friend, I am not being unfair to you. Did you not agree with me on one denarius?

verses.esv: Take what belongs to you and go. I choose to give to this last worker as I give to you.
verses.kjv: Take that thine is, and go thy way: I will give unto this last, even as unto thee.
verses.bsb: Take your pay and go. I want to give this last man the same as I gave you.

verses.esv: Am I not allowed to do what I choose with what belongs to me? Or do you begrudge my generosity?’
verses.kjv: Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? Is thine eye evil, because I am good?
verses.bsb: Do I not have the right to do as I please with what is mine? Or are you envious because I am generous?’

verses.esv: So the last will be first, and the first last.”
verses.kjv: So the last shall be first, and the first last: for many be called, but few chosen.
verses.bsb: So the last will be first, and the first will be last.”

verses.esv: And as Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside, and on the way he said to them,
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus going up to Jerusalem took the twelve disciples apart in the way, and said unto them,
verses.bsb: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, He took the twelve disciples aside and said,

verses.esv: “See, we are going up to Jerusalem. And the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death
verses.kjv: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the scribes, and they shall condemn him to death,
verses.bsb: “Look, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and scribes. They will condemn Him to death

verses.esv: and deliver him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified, and he will be raised on the third day.”
verses.kjv: And shall deliver him to the Gentiles to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify him: and the third day he shall rise again.
verses.bsb: and will deliver Him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified. And on the third day He will be raised to life.”

verses.esv: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came up to him with her sons, and kneeling before him she asked him for something.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then came to him the mother of Zebedee’s children with her sons, worshipping him, and desiring a certain thing of him.
verses.bsb: Then the mother of Zebedee’s sons came to Jesus with her sons and knelt down to make a request of Him.

verses.esv: And he said to her, “What do you want?” She said to him, “Say that these two sons of mine are to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your kingdom.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto her, What wilt thou? She saith unto him, Grant that these my two sons may sit, the one on thy right hand, and the other on the left, in thy kingdom.
verses.bsb: “What do you want?” He inquired.  She answered, “Declare that in Your kingdom one of these two sons of mine may sit at Your right hand, and the other at Your left.”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am to drink?” They said to him, “We are able.”
verses.kjv: But Jesus answered and said, Ye know not what ye ask. Are ye able to drink of the cup that I shall drink of, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with? They say unto him, We are able.
verses.bsb: “You do not know what you are asking,” Jesus replied. “Can you drink the cup I am going to drink?”  “We can,” the brothers answered.

verses.esv: He said to them, “You will drink my cup, but to sit at my right hand and at my left is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Ye shall drink indeed of my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with: but to sit on my right hand, and on my left, is not mine to give, but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared of my Father.
verses.bsb: “You will indeed drink My cup,” Jesus said. “But to sit at My right or left is not Mine to grant. These seats belong to those for whom My Father has prepared them.”

verses.esv: And when the ten heard it, they were indignant at the two brothers.
verses.kjv: And when the ten heard it, they were moved with indignation against the two brethren.
verses.bsb: When the ten heard about this, they were indignant with the two brothers.

verses.esv: But Jesus called them to him and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.
verses.kjv: But Jesus called them unto him, and said, Ye know that the princes of the Gentiles exercise dominion over them, and they that are great exercise authority upon them.
verses.bsb: But Jesus called them aside and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their superiors exercise authority over them.

verses.esv: It shall not be so among you. But whoever would be great among you must be your servant,
verses.kjv: But it shall not be so among you: but whosoever will be great among you, let him be your minister;
verses.bsb: It shall not be this way among you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant,

verses.esv: and whoever would be first among you must be your slave,
verses.kjv: And whosoever will be chief among you, let him be your servant:
verses.bsb: and whoever wants to be first among you must be your slave—

verses.esv: even as the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
verses.kjv: Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.
verses.bsb: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give His life as a ransom for many.”

verses.esv: And as they went out of Jericho, a great crowd followed him.
verses.kjv: And as they departed from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.
verses.bsb: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed Him.

verses.esv: And behold, there were two blind men sitting by the roadside, and when they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, two blind men sitting by the way side, when they heard that Jesus passed by, cried out, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou Son of David.
verses.bsb: And there were two blind men sitting beside the road. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!”

verses.esv: The crowd rebuked them, telling them to be silent, but they cried out all the more, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
verses.kjv: And the multitude rebuked them, because they should hold their peace: but they cried the more, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou Son of David.
verses.bsb: The crowd admonished them to be silent, but they cried out all the louder, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!”

verses.esv: And stopping, Jesus called them and said, “What do you want me to do for you?”
verses.kjv: And Jesus stood still, and called them, and said, What will ye that I shall do unto you?
verses.bsb: Jesus stopped and called them. “What do you want Me to do for you?” He asked.

verses.esv: They said to him, “Lord, let our eyes be opened.”
verses.kjv: They say unto him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.
verses.bsb: “Lord,” they answered, “let our eyes be opened.”

verses.esv: And Jesus in pity touched their eyes, and immediately they recovered their sight and followed him.
verses.kjv: So Jesus had compassion on them, and touched their eyes: and immediately their eyes received sight, and they followed him.
verses.bsb: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes, and at once they received their sight and followed Him.

verses.esv: Now when they drew near to Jerusalem and came to Bethphage, to the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,
verses.kjv: AND when they drew nigh unto Jerusalem, and were come to Bethphage, unto the mount of Olives, then sent Jesus two disciples,
verses.bsb: As they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent out two disciples,

verses.esv: saying to them, “Go into the village in front of you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her. Untie them and bring them to me.
verses.kjv: Saying unto them, Go into the village over against you, and straightway ye shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them unto me.
verses.bsb: saying to them, “Go into the village ahead of you, and at once you will find a donkey tied there, with her colt beside her. Untie them and bring them to Me.

verses.esv: If anyone says anything to you, you shall say, ‘The Lord needs them,’ and he will send them at once.”
verses.kjv: And if any man say ought unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of them; and straightway he will send them.
verses.bsb: If anyone questions you, tell him that the Lord needs them, and he will send them right away.”

verses.esv: This took place to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet, saying,
verses.kjv: All this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying,
verses.bsb: This took place to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet:

verses.esv: “Say to the daughter of Zion, ‘Behold, your king is coming to you, humble, and mounted on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a beast of burden.’”
verses.kjv: Tell ye the daughter of Sion, Behold, thy King cometh unto thee, meek, and sitting upon an ass, and a colt the foal of an ass.
verses.bsb: “Say to the Daughter of Zion, ‘See, your King comes to you, gentle and riding on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.’”

verses.esv: The disciples went and did as Jesus had directed them.
verses.kjv: And the disciples went, and did as Jesus commanded them,
verses.bsb: So the disciples went and did as Jesus had directed them.

verses.esv: They brought the donkey and the colt and put on them their cloaks, and he sat on them.
verses.kjv: And brought the ass, and the colt, and put on them their clothes, and they set him thereon.
verses.bsb: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their cloaks on them, and Jesus sat on them.

verses.esv: Most of the crowd spread their cloaks on the road, and others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
verses.kjv: And a very great multitude spread their garments in the way; others cut down branches from the trees, and strawed them in the way.
verses.bsb: A massive crowd spread their cloaks on the road, while others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.

verses.esv: And the crowds that went before him and that followed him were shouting, “Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”
verses.kjv: And the multitudes that went before, and that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna to the Son of David: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord; Hosanna in the highest.
verses.bsb: The crowds that went ahead of Him and those that followed were shouting:  “Hosanna to the Son of David!”   “Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord!”   “Hosanna in the highest!”

verses.esv: And when he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up, saying, “Who is this?”
verses.kjv: And when he was come into Jerusalem, all the city was moved, saying, Who is this?
verses.bsb: When Jesus had entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred and asked, “Who is this?”

verses.esv: And the crowds said, “This is the prophet Jesus, from Nazareth of Galilee.”
verses.kjv: And the multitude said, This is Jesus the prophet of Nazareth of Galilee.
verses.bsb: The crowds replied, “This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee.”

verses.esv: And Jesus entered the temple and drove out all who sold and bought in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money-changers and the seats of those who sold pigeons.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus went into the temple of God, and cast out all them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves,
verses.bsb: Then Jesus entered the temple courts and drove out all who were buying and selling there. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those selling doves.

verses.esv: He said to them, “It is written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer,’ but you make it a den of robbers.”
verses.kjv: And said unto them, It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.
verses.bsb: And He declared to them, “It is written: ‘My house will be called a house of prayer.’ But you are making it ‘a den of robbers.’”

verses.esv: And the blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he healed them.
verses.kjv: And the blind and the lame came to him in the temple; and he healed them.
verses.bsb: The blind and the lame came to Him at the temple, and He healed them.

verses.esv: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonderful things that he did, and the children crying out in the temple, “Hosanna to the Son of David!” they were indignant,
verses.kjv: And when the chief priests and scribes saw the wonderful things that he did, and the children crying in the temple, and saying, Hosanna to the Son of David; they were sore displeased,
verses.bsb: But the chief priests and scribes were indignant when they saw the wonders He performed and the children shouting in the temple courts, “Hosanna to the Son of David!”

verses.esv: and they said to him, “Do you hear what these are saying?” And Jesus said to them, “Yes; have you never read, “‘Out of the mouth of infants and nursing babies you have prepared praise’?”
verses.kjv: And said unto him, Hearest thou what these say? And Jesus saith unto them, Yea; have ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?
verses.bsb: “Do You hear what these children are saying?” they asked.  “Yes,” Jesus answered. “Have you never read:  ‘From the mouths of children and infants You have ordained praise’?”

verses.esv: And leaving them, he went out of the city to Bethany and lodged there.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he left them, and went out of the city into Bethany; and he lodged there.
verses.bsb: Then He left them and went out of the city to Bethany, where He spent the night.

verses.esv: In the morning, as he was returning to the city, he became hungry.
verses.kjv: Now in the morning as he returned into the city, he hungered.
verses.bsb: In the morning, as Jesus was returning to the city, He was hungry.

verses.esv: And seeing a fig tree by the wayside, he went to it and found nothing on it but only leaves. And he said to it, “May no fruit ever come from you again!” And the fig tree withered at once.
verses.kjv: And when he saw a fig tree in the way, he came to it, and found nothing thereon, but leaves only, and said unto it, Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever. And presently the fig tree withered away.
verses.bsb: Seeing a fig tree by the road, He went up to it but found nothing on it except leaves. “May you never bear fruit again!” He said. And immediately the tree withered.

verses.esv: When the disciples saw it, they marveled, saying, “How did the fig tree wither at once?”
verses.kjv: And when the disciples saw it, they marvelled, saying, How soon is the fig tree withered away!
verses.bsb: When the disciples saw this, they marveled and asked, “How did the fig tree wither so quickly?”

verses.esv: And Jesus answered them, “Truly, I say to you, if you have faith and do not doubt, you will not only do what has been done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea,’ it will happen.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Verily I say unto you, If ye have faith, and doubt not, ye shall not only do this which is done to the fig tree, but also if ye shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; it shall be done.
verses.bsb: “Truly I tell you,” Jesus replied, “if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ it will happen.

verses.esv: And whatever you ask in prayer, you will receive, if you have faith.”
verses.kjv: And all things, whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.
verses.bsb: If you believe, you will receive whatever you ask for in prayer.”

verses.esv: And when he entered the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came up to him as he was teaching, and said, “By what authority are you doing these things, and who gave you this authority?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he was come into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came unto him as he was teaching, and said, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority?
verses.bsb: When Jesus returned to the temple courts and began to teach, the chief priests and elders of the people came up to Him. “By what authority are You doing these things?” they asked. “And who gave You this authority?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered them, “I also will ask you one question, and if you tell me the answer, then I also will tell you by what authority I do these things.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, I also will ask you one thing, which if ye tell me, I in like wise will tell you by what authority I do these things.
verses.bsb: “I will also ask you one question,” Jesus replied, “and if you answer Me, I will tell you by what authority I am doing these things.

verses.esv: The baptism of John, from where did it come? From heaven or from man?” And they discussed it among themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say to us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
verses.kjv: The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven, or of men? And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say unto us, Why did ye not then believe him?
verses.bsb: What was the source of John’s baptism? Was it from heaven or from men?”  They deliberated among themselves and said, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ He will ask, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’

verses.esv: But if we say, ‘From man,’ we are afraid of the crowd, for they all hold that John was a prophet.”
verses.kjv: But if we shall say, Of men; we fear the people; for all hold John as a prophet.
verses.bsb: But if we say, ‘From men,’ we are afraid of the people, for they all regard John as a prophet.”

verses.esv: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And he said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
verses.kjv: And they answered Jesus, and said, We cannot tell. And he said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
verses.bsb: So they answered, “We do not know.”  And Jesus replied, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.

verses.esv: “What do you think? A man had two sons. And he went to the first and said, ‘Son, go and work in the vineyard today.’
verses.kjv: ¶ But what think ye? A certain man had two sons; and he came to the first, and said, Son, go work to day in my vineyard.
verses.bsb: But what do you think? There was a man who had two sons. He went to the first one and said, ‘Son, go and work today in the vineyard.’

verses.esv: And he answered, ‘I will not,’ but afterward he changed his mind and went.
verses.kjv: He answered and said, I will not: but afterward he repented, and went.
verses.bsb: ‘I will not,’ he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.

verses.esv: And he went to the other son and said the same. And he answered, ‘I go, sir,’ but did not go.
verses.kjv: And he came to the second, and said likewise. And he answered and said, I go, sir: and went not.
verses.bsb: Then the man went to the second son and told him the same thing.  ‘I will, sir,’ he said. But he did not go.

verses.esv: Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Truly, I say to you, the tax collectors and the prostitutes go into the kingdom of God before you.
verses.kjv: Whether of them twain did the will of his father? They say unto him, The first. Jesus saith unto them, Verily I say unto you, That the publicans and the harlots go into the kingdom of God before you.
verses.bsb: Which of the two did the will of his father?”  “The first,” they answered.  Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, the tax collectors and prostitutes are entering the kingdom of God before you.

verses.esv: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes believed him. And even when you saw it, you did not afterward change your minds and believe him.
verses.kjv: For John came unto you in the way of righteousness, and ye believed him not: but the publicans and the harlots believed him: and ye, when ye had seen it, repented not afterward, that ye might believe him.
verses.bsb: For John came to you in a righteous way and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and prostitutes did. And even after you saw this, you did not repent and believe him.

verses.esv: “Hear another parable. There was a master of a house who planted a vineyard and put a fence around it and dug a winepress in it and built a tower and leased it to tenants, and went into another country.
verses.kjv: ¶ Hear another parable: There was a certain householder, which planted a vineyard, and hedged it round about, and digged a winepress in it, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country:
verses.bsb: Listen to another parable: There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it out to some tenants and went away on a journey.

verses.esv: When the season for fruit drew near, he sent his servants to the tenants to get his fruit.
verses.kjv: And when the time of the fruit drew near, he sent his servants to the husbandmen, that they might receive the fruits of it.
verses.bsb: When the harvest time drew near, he sent his servants to the tenants to collect his share of the fruit.

verses.esv: And the tenants took his servants and beat one, killed another, and stoned another.
verses.kjv: And the husbandmen took his servants, and beat one, and killed another, and stoned another.
verses.bsb: But the tenants seized his servants. They beat one, killed another, and stoned a third.

verses.esv: Again he sent other servants, more than the first. And they did the same to them.
verses.kjv: Again, he sent other servants more than the first: and they did unto them likewise.
verses.bsb: Again, he sent other servants, more than the first group. But the tenants did the same to them.

verses.esv: Finally he sent his son to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
verses.kjv: But last of all he sent unto them his son, saying, They will reverence my son.
verses.bsb: Finally, he sent his son to them. ‘They will respect my son,’ he said.

verses.esv: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him and have his inheritance.’
verses.kjv: But when the husbandmen saw the son, they said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and let us seize on his inheritance.
verses.bsb: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him and take his inheritance.’

verses.esv: And they took him and threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
verses.kjv: And they caught him, and cast him out of the vineyard, and slew him.
verses.bsb: So they seized him and threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.

verses.esv: When therefore the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?”
verses.kjv: When the lord therefore of the vineyard cometh, what will he do unto those husbandmen?
verses.bsb: Therefore, when the owner of the vineyard returns, what will he do to those tenants?”

verses.esv: They said to him, “He will put those wretches to a miserable death and let out the vineyard to other tenants who will give him the fruits in their seasons.”
verses.kjv: They say unto him, He will miserably destroy those wicked men, and will let out his vineyard unto other husbandmen, which shall render him the fruits in their seasons.
verses.bsb: “He will bring those wretches to a wretched end,” they replied, “and will rent out the vineyard to other tenants who will give him his share of the fruit at harvest time.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the Scriptures: “‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone; this was the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes’?
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Did ye never read in the scriptures, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner: this is the Lord’s doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?
verses.bsb: Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the Scriptures:  ‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. This is from the Lord, and it is marvelous in our eyes’?

verses.esv: Therefore I tell you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people producing its fruits.
verses.kjv: Therefore say I unto you, The kingdom of God shall be taken from you, and given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof.
verses.bsb: Therefore I tell you that the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people who will produce its fruit.

verses.esv: And the one who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces; and when it falls on anyone, it will crush him.”
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken: but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.
verses.bsb: He who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, but he on whom it falls will be crushed.”

verses.esv: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they perceived that he was speaking about them.
verses.kjv: And when the chief priests and Pharisees had heard his parables, they perceived that he spake of them.
verses.bsb: When the chief priests and Pharisees heard His parables, they knew that Jesus was speaking about them.

verses.esv: And although they were seeking to arrest him, they feared the crowds, because they held him to be a prophet.
verses.kjv: But when they sought to lay hands on him, they feared the multitude, because they took him for a prophet.
verses.bsb: Although they wanted to arrest Him, they were afraid of the crowds, because the people regarded Him as a prophet.

verses.esv: And again Jesus spoke to them in parables, saying,
verses.kjv: AND Jesus answered and spake unto them again by parables, and said,
verses.bsb: Once again, Jesus spoke to them in parables:

verses.esv: “The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a king who gave a wedding feast for his son,
verses.kjv: The kingdom of heaven is like unto a certain king, which made a marriage for his son,
verses.bsb: “The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding banquet for his son.

verses.esv: and sent his servants to call those who were invited to the wedding feast, but they would not come.
verses.kjv: And sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the wedding: and they would not come.
verses.bsb: He sent his servants to call those he had invited to the banquet, but they refused to come.

verses.esv: Again he sent other servants, saying, ‘Tell those who are invited, “See, I have prepared my dinner, my oxen and my fat calves have been slaughtered, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding feast.”’
verses.kjv: Again, he sent forth other servants, saying, Tell them which are bidden, Behold, I have prepared my dinner: my oxen and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come unto the marriage.
verses.bsb: Again, he sent other servants and said, ‘Tell those who have been invited that I have prepared my dinner. My oxen and fattened cattle have been killed, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding banquet.’

verses.esv: But they paid no attention and went off, one to his farm, another to his business,
verses.kjv: But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his farm, another to his merchandise:
verses.bsb: But they paid no attention and went away, one to his field, another to his business.

verses.esv: while the rest seized his servants, treated them shamefully, and killed them.
verses.kjv: And the remnant took his servants, and entreated them spitefully, and slew them.
verses.bsb: The rest seized his servants, mistreated them, and killed them.

verses.esv: The king was angry, and he sent his troops and destroyed those murderers and burned their city.
verses.kjv: But when the king heard thereof, he was wroth: and he sent forth his armies, and destroyed those murderers, and burned up their city.
verses.bsb: The king was enraged, and he sent his troops to destroy those murderers and burn their city.

verses.esv: Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding feast is ready, but those invited were not worthy.
verses.kjv: Then saith he to his servants, The wedding is ready, but they which were bidden were not worthy.
verses.bsb: Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding banquet is ready, but those I invited were not worthy.

verses.esv: Go therefore to the main roads and invite to the wedding feast as many as you find.’
verses.kjv: Go ye therefore into the highways, and as many as ye shall find, bid to the marriage.
verses.bsb: Go therefore to the crossroads and invite to the banquet as many as you can find.’

verses.esv: And those servants went out into the roads and gathered all whom they found, both bad and good. So the wedding hall was filled with guests.
verses.kjv: So those servants went out into the highways, and gathered together all as many as they found, both bad and good: and the wedding was furnished with guests.
verses.bsb: So the servants went out into the streets and gathered everyone they could find, both evil and good, and the wedding hall was filled with guests.

verses.esv: “But when the king came in to look at the guests, he saw there a man who had no wedding garment.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man which had not on a wedding garment:
verses.bsb: But when the king came in to see the guests, he spotted a man who was not dressed in wedding clothes.

verses.esv: And he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you get in here without a wedding garment?’ And he was speechless.
verses.kjv: And he saith unto him, Friend, how camest thou in hither not having a wedding garment? And he was speechless.
verses.bsb: ‘Friend,’ he asked, ‘how did you get in here without wedding clothes?’  But the man was speechless.

verses.esv: Then the king said to the attendants, ‘Bind him hand and foot and cast him into the outer darkness. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
verses.kjv: Then said the king to the servants, Bind him hand and foot, and take him away, and cast him into outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
verses.bsb: Then the king told the servants, ‘Tie him hand and foot, and throw him into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’

verses.esv: For many are called, but few are chosen.”
verses.kjv: For many are called, but few are chosen.
verses.bsb: For many are called, but few are chosen.”

verses.esv: Then the Pharisees went and plotted how to entangle him in his words.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then went the Pharisees, and took counsel how they might entangle him in his talk.
verses.bsb: Then the Pharisees went out and conspired to trap Jesus in His words.

verses.esv: And they sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying, “Teacher, we know that you are true and teach the way of God truthfully, and you do not care about anyone's opinion, for you are not swayed by appearances.
verses.kjv: And they sent out unto him their disciples with the Herodians, saying, Master, we know that thou art true, and teachest the way of God in truth, neither carest thou for any man: for thou regardest not the person of men.
verses.bsb: They sent their disciples to Him along with the Herodians. “Teacher,” they said, “we know that You are honest and that You teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. You seek favor from no one, because You pay no attention to external appearance.

verses.esv: Tell us, then, what you think. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”
verses.kjv: Tell us therefore, What thinkest thou? Is it lawful to give tribute unto Cæsar, or not?
verses.bsb: So tell us what You think: Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”

verses.esv: But Jesus, aware of their malice, said, “Why put me to the test, you hypocrites?
verses.kjv: But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said, Why tempt ye me, ye hypocrites?
verses.bsb: But Jesus knew their evil intent and said, “You hypocrites, why are you testing Me?

verses.esv: Show me the coin for the tax.” And they brought him a denarius.
verses.kjv: Shew me the tribute money. And they brought unto him a penny.
verses.bsb: Show Me the coin used for the tax.”  And they brought Him a denarius.

verses.esv: And Jesus said to them, “Whose likeness and inscription is this?”
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription?
verses.bsb: “Whose image is this,” He asked, “and whose inscription?”

verses.esv: They said, “Caesar's.” Then he said to them, “Therefore render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.”
verses.kjv: They say unto him, Cæsar’s. Then saith he unto them, Render therefore unto Cæsar the things which are Cæsar’s; and unto God the things that are God’s.
verses.bsb: “Caesar’s,” they answered.  So Jesus told them, “Give to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.”

verses.esv: When they heard it, they marveled. And they left him and went away.
verses.kjv: When they had heard these words, they marvelled, and left him, and went their way.
verses.bsb: And when they heard this, they were amazed. So they left Him and went away.

verses.esv: The same day Sadducees came to him, who say that there is no resurrection, and they asked him a question,
verses.kjv: ¶ The same day came to him the Sadducees, which say that there is no resurrection, and asked him,
verses.bsb: That same day the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Jesus and questioned Him.

verses.esv: saying, “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies having no children, his brother must marry the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.’
verses.kjv: Saying, Master, Moses said, If a man die, having no children, his brother shall marry his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
verses.bsb: “Teacher,” they said, “Moses declared that if a man dies without having children, his brother is to marry the widow and raise up offspring for him.

verses.esv: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first married and died, and having no offspring left his wife to his brother.
verses.kjv: Now there were with us seven brethren: and the first, when he had married a wife, deceased, and, having no issue, left his wife unto his brother:
verses.bsb: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married and died without having children. So he left his wife to his brother.

verses.esv: So too the second and third, down to the seventh.
verses.kjv: Likewise the second also, and the third, unto the seventh.
verses.bsb: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers, down to the seventh.

verses.esv: After them all, the woman died.
verses.kjv: And last of all the woman died also.
verses.bsb: And last of all, the woman died.

verses.esv: In the resurrection, therefore, of the seven, whose wife will she be? For they all had her.”
verses.kjv: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife shall she be of the seven? for they all had her.
verses.bsb: In the resurrection, then, whose wife will she be of the seven? For all of them were married to her.”

verses.esv: But Jesus answered them, “You are wrong, because you know neither the Scriptures nor the power of God.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Ye do err, not knowing the scriptures, nor the power of God.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “You are mistaken because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God.

verses.esv: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
verses.kjv: For in the resurrection they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are as the angels of God in heaven.
verses.bsb: In the resurrection, people will neither marry nor be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like the angels in heaven.

verses.esv: And as for the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was said to you by God:
verses.kjv: But as touching the resurrection of the dead, have ye not read that which was spoken unto you by God, saying,
verses.bsb: But concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what God said to you:

verses.esv: ‘I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is not God of the dead, but of the living.”
verses.kjv: I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.
verses.bsb: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is not the God of the dead, but of the living.”

verses.esv: And when the crowd heard it, they were astonished at his teaching.
verses.kjv: And when the multitude heard this, they were astonished at his doctrine.
verses.bsb: When the crowds heard this, they were astonished at His teaching.

verses.esv: But when the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together.
verses.kjv: ¶ But when the Pharisees had heard that he had put the Sadducees to silence, they were gathered together.
verses.bsb: And when the Pharisees heard that Jesus had silenced the Sadducees, they themselves gathered together.

verses.esv: And one of them, a lawyer, asked him a question to test him.
verses.kjv: Then one of them, which was a lawyer, asked him a question, tempting him, and saying,
verses.bsb: One of them, an expert in the law, tested Him with a question:

verses.esv: “Teacher, which is the great commandment in the Law?”
verses.kjv: Master, which is the great commandment in the law?
verses.bsb: “Teacher, which commandment is the greatest in the Law?”

verses.esv: And he said to him, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto him, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind.
verses.bsb: Jesus declared, “‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.’

verses.esv: This is the great and first commandment.
verses.kjv: This is the first and great commandment.
verses.bsb: This is the first and greatest commandment.

verses.esv: And a second is like it: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
verses.kjv: And the second is like unto it, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
verses.bsb: And the second is like it: ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’

verses.esv: On these two commandments depend all the Law and the Prophets.”
verses.kjv: On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.
verses.bsb: All the Law and the Prophets hang on these two commandments.”

verses.esv: Now while the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them a question,
verses.kjv: ¶ While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them,
verses.bsb: While the Pharisees were assembled, Jesus questioned them:

verses.esv: saying, “What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he?” They said to him, “The son of David.”
verses.kjv: Saying, What think ye of Christ? whose son is he? They say unto him, The Son of David.
verses.bsb: “What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is He?”  “David’s,” they answered.

verses.esv: He said to them, “How is it then that David, in the Spirit, calls him Lord, saying,
verses.kjv: He saith unto them, How then doth David in spirit call him Lord, saying,
verses.bsb: Jesus said to them, “How then does David in the Spirit call Him ‘Lord’? For he says:

verses.esv: “‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet”’?
verses.kjv: The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool?
verses.bsb: ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand until I put Your enemies under Your feet.”’

verses.esv: If then David calls him Lord, how is he his son?”
verses.kjv: If David then call him Lord, how is he his son?
verses.bsb: So if David calls Him ‘Lord,’ how can He be David’s son?”

verses.esv: And no one was able to answer him a word, nor from that day did anyone dare to ask him any more questions.
verses.kjv: And no man was able to answer him a word, neither durst any man from that day forth ask him any more questions.
verses.bsb: No one was able to answer a word, and from that day on no one dared to question Him any further.

verses.esv: Then Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples,
verses.kjv: THEN spake Jesus to the multitude, and to his disciples,
verses.bsb: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and to His disciples:

verses.esv: “The scribes and the Pharisees sit on Moses' seat,
verses.kjv: Saying, The scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses’ seat:
verses.bsb: “The scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses’ seat.

verses.esv: so do and observe whatever they tell you, but not the works they do. For they preach, but do not practice.
verses.kjv: All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not ye after their works: for they say, and do not.
verses.bsb: So practice and observe everything they tell you. But do not do what they do, for they do not practice what they preach.

verses.esv: They tie up heavy burdens, hard to bear, and lay them on people's shoulders, but they themselves are not willing to move them with their finger.
verses.kjv: For they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers.
verses.bsb: They tie up heavy, burdensome loads and lay them on men’s shoulders, but they themselves are not willing to lift a finger to move them.

verses.esv: They do all their deeds to be seen by others. For they make their phylacteries broad and their fringes long,
verses.kjv: But all their works they do for to be seen of men: they make broad their phylacteries, and enlarge the borders of their garments,
verses.bsb: All their deeds are done for men to see. They broaden their phylacteries and lengthen their tassels.

verses.esv: and they love the place of honor at feasts and the best seats in the synagogues
verses.kjv: And love the uppermost rooms at feasts, and the chief seats in the synagogues,
verses.bsb: They love the places of honor at banquets, the chief seats in the synagogues,

verses.esv: and greetings in the marketplaces and being called rabbi by others.
verses.kjv: And greetings in the markets, and to be called of men, Rabbi, Rabbi.
verses.bsb: the greetings in the marketplaces, and the title of ‘Rabbi’ by which they are addressed.

verses.esv: But you are not to be called rabbi, for you have one teacher, and you are all brothers.
verses.kjv: But be not ye called Rabbi: for one is your Master, even Christ; and all ye are brethren.
verses.bsb: But you are not to be called ‘Rabbi,’ for you have one Teacher, and you are all brothers.

verses.esv: And call no man your father on earth, for you have one Father, who is in heaven.
verses.kjv: And call no man your father upon the earth: for one is your Father, which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: And do not call anyone on earth your father, for you have one Father, who is in heaven.

verses.esv: Neither be called instructors, for you have one instructor, the Christ.
verses.kjv: Neither be ye called masters: for one is your Master, even Christ.
verses.bsb: Nor are you to be called instructors, for you have one Instructor, the Christ.

verses.esv: The greatest among you shall be your servant.
verses.kjv: But he that is greatest among you shall be your servant.
verses.bsb: The greatest among you shall be your servant.

verses.esv: Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased; and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.
verses.bsb: For whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.

verses.esv: “But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you shut the kingdom of heaven in people's faces. For you neither enter yourselves nor allow those who would enter to go in.
verses.kjv: ¶ But woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye shut up the kingdom of heaven against men: for ye neither go in yourselves, neither suffer ye them that are entering to go in.
verses.bsb: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You shut the kingdom of heaven in men’s faces. You yourselves do not enter, nor will you let in those who wish to enter.

verses.esv: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel across sea and land to make a single proselyte, and when he becomes a proselyte, you make him twice as much a child of hell as yourselves.
verses.kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte, and when he is made, ye make him twofold more the child of hell than yourselves.
verses.bsb: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You traverse land and sea to win a single convert, and when he becomes one, you make him twice as much a son of hell as you are.

verses.esv: “Woe to you, blind guides, who say, ‘If anyone swears by the temple, it is nothing, but if anyone swears by the gold of the temple, he is bound by his oath.’
verses.kjv: Woe unto you, ye blind guides, which say, Whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gold of the temple, he is a debtor!
verses.bsb: Woe to you, blind guides! You say, ‘If anyone swears by the temple, it means nothing; but if anyone swears by the gold of the temple, he is bound by his oath.’

verses.esv: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gold or the temple that has made the gold sacred?
verses.kjv: Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifieth the gold?
verses.bsb: You blind fools! Which is greater: the gold, or the temple that makes it sacred?

verses.esv: And you say, ‘If anyone swears by the altar, it is nothing, but if anyone swears by the gift that is on the altar, he is bound by his oath.’
verses.kjv: And, Whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whosoever sweareth by the gift that is upon it, he is guilty.
verses.bsb: And you say, ‘If anyone swears by the altar, it means nothing; but if anyone swears by the gift on it, he is bound by his oath.’

verses.esv: You blind men! For which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
verses.kjv: Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifieth the gift?
verses.bsb: You blind men! Which is greater: the gift, or the altar that makes it sacred?

verses.esv: So whoever swears by the altar swears by it and by everything on it.
verses.kjv: Whoso therefore shall swear by the altar, sweareth by it, and by all things thereon.
verses.bsb: So then, he who swears by the altar swears by it and by everything on it.

verses.esv: And whoever swears by the temple swears by it and by him who dwells in it.
verses.kjv: And whoso shall swear by the temple, sweareth by it, and by him that dwelleth therein.
verses.bsb: And he who swears by the temple swears by it and by the One who dwells in it.

verses.esv: And whoever swears by heaven swears by the throne of God and by him who sits upon it.
verses.kjv: And he that shall swear by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.
verses.bsb: And he who swears by heaven swears by God’s throne and by the One who sits on it.

verses.esv: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint and dill and cumin, and have neglected the weightier matters of the law: justice and mercy and faithfulness. These you ought to have done, without neglecting the others.
verses.kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye pay tithe of mint and anise and cummin, and have omitted the weightier matters of the law, judgment, mercy, and faith: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.
verses.bsb: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You pay tithes of mint, dill, and cumin. But you have disregarded the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faithfulness. You should have practiced the latter, without neglecting the former.

verses.esv: You blind guides, straining out a gnat and swallowing a camel!
verses.kjv: Ye blind guides, which strain at a gnat, and swallow a camel.
verses.bsb: You blind guides! You strain out a gnat but swallow a camel.

verses.esv: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and the plate, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
verses.kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye make clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and excess.
verses.bsb: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.

verses.esv: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup and the plate, that the outside also may be clean.
verses.kjv: Thou blind Pharisee, cleanse first that which is within the cup and platter, that the outside of them may be clean also.
verses.bsb: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup and dish, so that the outside may become clean as well.

verses.esv: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitewashed tombs, which outwardly appear beautiful, but within are full of dead people's bones and all uncleanness.
verses.kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are like unto whited sepulchres, which indeed appear beautiful outward, but are within full of dead men’s bones, and of all uncleanness.
verses.bsb: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside, but on the inside are full of dead men’s bones and every kind of impurity.

verses.esv: So you also outwardly appear righteous to others, but within you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
verses.kjv: Even so ye also outwardly appear righteous unto men, but within ye are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.
verses.bsb: In the same way, on the outside you appear to be righteous, but on the inside you are full of hypocrisy and wickedness.

verses.esv: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets and decorate the monuments of the righteous,
verses.kjv: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye build the tombs of the prophets, and garnish the sepulchres of the righteous,
verses.bsb: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the monuments of the righteous.

verses.esv: saying, ‘If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we would not have taken part with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.’
verses.kjv: And say, If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.
verses.bsb: And you say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partners with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.’

verses.esv: Thus you witness against yourselves that you are sons of those who murdered the prophets.
verses.kjv: Wherefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves, that ye are the children of them which killed the prophets.
verses.bsb: So you testify against yourselves that you are the sons of those who murdered the prophets.

verses.esv: Fill up, then, the measure of your fathers.
verses.kjv: Fill ye up then the measure of your fathers.
verses.bsb: Fill up, then, the measure of the sin of your fathers.

verses.esv: You serpents, you brood of vipers, how are you to escape being sentenced to hell?
verses.kjv: Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers, how can ye escape the damnation of hell?
verses.bsb: You snakes! You brood of vipers! How will you escape the sentence of hell?

verses.esv: Therefore I send you prophets and wise men and scribes, some of whom you will kill and crucify, and some you will flog in your synagogues and persecute from town to town,
verses.kjv: ¶ Wherefore, behold, I send unto you prophets, and wise men, and scribes: and some of them ye shall kill and crucify; and some of them shall ye scourge in your synagogues, and persecute them from city to city:
verses.bsb: Because of this, I am sending you prophets and wise men and teachers. Some of them you will kill and crucify, and others you will flog in your synagogues and persecute in town after town.

verses.esv: so that on you may come all the righteous blood shed on earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you murdered between the sanctuary and the altar.
verses.kjv: That upon you may come all the righteous blood shed upon the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel unto the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom ye slew between the temple and the altar.
verses.bsb: And so upon you will come all the righteous blood shed on earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah son of Berechiah, whom you murdered between the temple and the altar.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, all these things will come upon this generation.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, All these things shall come upon this generation.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, all these things will come upon this generation.

verses.esv: “O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the city that kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to it! How often would I have gathered your children together as a hen gathers her brood under her wings, and you were not willing!
verses.kjv: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets, and stonest them which are sent unto thee, how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, and ye would not!
verses.bsb: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those sent to her, how often I have longed to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you were unwilling!

verses.esv: See, your house is left to you desolate.
verses.kjv: Behold, your house is left unto you desolate.
verses.bsb: Look, your house is left to you desolate.

verses.esv: For I tell you, you will not see me again, until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.’”
verses.kjv: For I say unto you, Ye shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: For I tell you that you will not see Me again until you say, ‘Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord.’”

verses.esv: Jesus left the temple and was going away, when his disciples came to point out to him the buildings of the temple.
verses.kjv: AND Jesus went out, and departed from the temple: and his disciples came to him for to shew him the buildings of the temple.
verses.bsb: As Jesus left the temple and was walking away, His disciples came up to Him to point out its buildings.

verses.esv: But he answered them, “You see all these, do you not? Truly, I say to you, there will not be left here one stone upon another that will not be thrown down.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, See ye not all these things? verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
verses.bsb: “Do you see all these things?” He replied. “Truly I tell you, not one stone here will be left on another; every one will be thrown down.”

verses.esv: As he sat on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately, saying, “Tell us, when will these things be, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And as he sat upon the mount of Olives, the disciples came unto him privately, saying, Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign of thy coming, and of the end of the world?
verses.bsb: While Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to Him privately. “Tell us,” they said, “when will these things happen, and what will be the sign of Your coming and of the end of the age?”

verses.esv: And Jesus answered them, “See that no one leads you astray.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man deceive you.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “See to it that no one deceives you.

verses.esv: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Christ,’ and they will lead many astray.
verses.kjv: For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.
verses.bsb: For many will come in My name, claiming, ‘I am the Christ,’ and will deceive many.

verses.esv: And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you are not alarmed, for this must take place, but the end is not yet.
verses.kjv: And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
verses.bsb: You will hear of wars and rumors of wars, but see to it that you are not alarmed. These things must happen, but the end is still to come.

verses.esv: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
verses.kjv: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places.
verses.bsb: Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be famines and earthquakes in various places.

verses.esv: All these are but the beginning of the birth pains.
verses.kjv: All these are the beginning of sorrows.
verses.bsb: All these are the beginning of birth pains.

verses.esv: “Then they will deliver you up to tribulation and put you to death, and you will be hated by all nations for my name's sake.
verses.kjv: Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted, and shall kill you: and ye shall be hated of all nations for my name’s sake.
verses.bsb: Then they will deliver you over to be persecuted and killed, and you will be hated by all nations because of My name.

verses.esv: And then many will fall away and betray one another and hate one another.
verses.kjv: And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.
verses.bsb: At that time many will fall away and will betray and hate one another,

verses.esv: And many false prophets will arise and lead many astray.
verses.kjv: And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.
verses.bsb: and many false prophets will arise and mislead many.

verses.esv: And because lawlessness will be increased, the love of many will grow cold.
verses.kjv: And because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.
verses.bsb: Because of the multiplication of wickedness, the love of most will grow cold.

verses.esv: But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
verses.kjv: But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.
verses.bsb: But the one who perseveres to the end will be saved.

verses.esv: And this gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the whole world as a testimony to all nations, and then the end will come.
verses.kjv: And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.
verses.bsb: And this gospel of the kingdom will be preached in all the world as a testimony to all nations, and then the end will come.

verses.esv: “So when you see the abomination of desolation spoken of by the prophet Daniel, standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),
verses.kjv: When ye therefore shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place, (whoso readeth, let him understand:)
verses.bsb: So when you see standing in the holy place ‘the abomination of desolation,’ described by the prophet Daniel (let the reader understand),

verses.esv: then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
verses.kjv: Then let them which be in Judæa flee into the mountains:
verses.bsb: then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.

verses.esv: Let the one who is on the housetop not go down to take what is in his house,
verses.kjv: Let him which is on the housetop not come down to take any thing out of his house:
verses.bsb: Let no one on the housetop come down to retrieve anything from his house.

verses.esv: and let the one who is in the field not turn back to take his cloak.
verses.kjv: Neither let him which is in the field return back to take his clothes.
verses.bsb: And let no one in the field return for his cloak.

verses.esv: And alas for women who are pregnant and for those who are nursing infants in those days!
verses.kjv: And woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!
verses.bsb: How miserable those days will be for pregnant and nursing mothers!

verses.esv: Pray that your flight may not be in winter or on a Sabbath.
verses.kjv: But pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, neither on the sabbath day:
verses.bsb: Pray that your flight will not occur in the winter or on the Sabbath.

verses.esv: For then there will be great tribulation, such as has not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, and never will be.
verses.kjv: For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor ever shall be.
verses.bsb: For at that time there will be great tribulation, unmatched from the beginning of the world until now, and never to be seen again.

verses.esv: And if those days had not been cut short, no human being would be saved. But for the sake of the elect those days will be cut short.
verses.kjv: And except those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved: but for the elect’s sake those days shall be shortened.
verses.bsb: If those days had not been cut short, nobody would be saved. But for the sake of the elect, those days will be cut short.

verses.esv: Then if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or ‘There he is!’ do not believe it.
verses.kjv: Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there; believe it not.
verses.bsb: At that time, if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or ‘There He is!’ do not believe it.

verses.esv: For false christs and false prophets will arise and perform great signs and wonders, so as to lead astray, if possible, even the elect.
verses.kjv: For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall shew great signs and wonders; insomuch that, if it were possible, they shall deceive the very elect.
verses.bsb: For false Christs and false prophets will appear and perform great signs and wonders that would deceive even the elect, if that were possible.

verses.esv: See, I have told you beforehand.
verses.kjv: Behold, I have told you before.
verses.bsb: See, I have told you in advance.

verses.esv: So, if they say to you, ‘Look, he is in the wilderness,’ do not go out. If they say, ‘Look, he is in the inner rooms,’ do not believe it.
verses.kjv: Wherefore if they shall say unto you, Behold, he is in the desert; go not forth: behold, he is in the secret chambers; believe it not.
verses.bsb: So if they tell you, ‘There He is in the wilderness,’ do not go out; or, ‘Here He is in the inner rooms,’ do not believe it.

verses.esv: For as the lightning comes from the east and shines as far as the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
verses.kjv: For as the lightning cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
verses.bsb: For just as the lightning comes from the east and flashes as far as the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.

verses.esv: Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.
verses.kjv: For wheresoever the carcase is, there will the eagles be gathered together.
verses.bsb: Wherever there is a carcass, there the vultures will gather.

verses.esv: “Immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light, and the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
verses.kjv: ¶ Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken:
verses.bsb: Immediately after the tribulation of those days:  ‘The sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light; the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.’

verses.esv: Then will appear in heaven the sign of the Son of Man, and then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
verses.kjv: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
verses.bsb: At that time the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn. They will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven, with power and great glory.

verses.esv: And he will send out his angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
verses.kjv: And he shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
verses.bsb: And He will send out His angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather His elect from the four winds, from one end of the heavens to the other.

verses.esv: “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts out its leaves, you know that summer is near.
verses.kjv: Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh:
verses.bsb: Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its branches become tender and sprout leaves, you know that summer is near.

verses.esv: So also, when you see all these things, you know that he is near, at the very gates.
verses.kjv: So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.
verses.bsb: So also, when you see all these things, you will know that He is near, right at the door.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass, till all these things be fulfilled.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have happened.

verses.esv: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
verses.kjv: Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away.
verses.bsb: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will never pass away.

verses.esv: “But concerning that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels of heaven, nor the Son, but the Father only.
verses.kjv: ¶ But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but my Father only.
verses.bsb: No one knows about that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.

verses.esv: For as were the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
verses.kjv: But as the days of Noe were, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
verses.bsb: As it was in the days of Noah, so will it be at the coming of the Son of Man.

verses.esv: For as in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day when Noah entered the ark,
verses.kjv: For as in the days that were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark,
verses.bsb: For in the days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, up to the day Noah entered the ark.

verses.esv: and they were unaware until the flood came and swept them all away, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
verses.kjv: And knew not until the flood came, and took them all away; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
verses.bsb: And they were oblivious, until the flood came and swept them all away. So will it be at the coming of the Son of Man.

verses.esv: Then two men will be in the field; one will be taken and one left.
verses.kjv: Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
verses.bsb: Two men will be in the field: one will be taken and the other left.

verses.esv: Two women will be grinding at the mill; one will be taken and one left.
verses.kjv: Two women shall be grinding at the mill; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
verses.bsb: Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and the other left.

verses.esv: Therefore, stay awake, for you do not know on what day your Lord is coming.
verses.kjv: ¶ Watch therefore: for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.
verses.bsb: Therefore keep watch, because you do not know the day on which your Lord will come.

verses.esv: But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what part of the night the thief was coming, he would have stayed awake and would not have let his house be broken into.
