verses.kjv: But know this, that if the goodman of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken up.
verses.bsb: But understand this: If the homeowner had known in which watch of the night the thief was coming, he would have kept watch and would not have let his house be broken into.

verses.esv: Therefore you also must be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.
verses.kjv: Therefore be ye also ready: for in such an hour as ye think not the Son of man cometh.
verses.bsb: For this reason, you also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour you do not expect.

verses.esv: “Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his master has set over his household, to give them their food at the proper time?
verses.kjv: Who then is a faithful and wise servant, whom his lord hath made ruler over his household, to give them meat in due season?
verses.bsb: Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom the master has put in charge of his household, to give the others their food at the proper time?

verses.esv: Blessed is that servant whom his master will find so doing when he comes.
verses.kjv: Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.
verses.bsb: Blessed is that servant whose master finds him doing so when he returns.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, he will set him over all his possessions.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, That he shall make him ruler over all his goods.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.

verses.esv: But if that wicked servant says to himself, ‘My master is delayed,’
verses.kjv: But and if that evil servant shall say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming;
verses.bsb: But suppose that servant is wicked and says in his heart, ‘My master will be away a long time.’

verses.esv: and begins to beat his fellow servants and eats and drinks with drunkards,
verses.kjv: And shall begin to smite his fellowservants, and to eat and drink with the drunken;
verses.bsb: And he begins to beat his fellow servants and to eat and drink with drunkards.

verses.esv: the master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not know
verses.kjv: The lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him, and in an hour that he is not aware of,
verses.bsb: The master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not anticipate.

verses.esv: and will cut him in pieces and put him with the hypocrites. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
verses.kjv: And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
verses.bsb: Then he will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

verses.esv: “Then the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom.
verses.kjv: THEN shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom.
verses.bsb: “At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.

verses.esv: Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.
verses.kjv: And five of them were wise, and five were foolish.
verses.bsb: Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.

verses.esv: For when the foolish took their lamps, they took no oil with them,
verses.kjv: They that were foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them:
verses.bsb: The foolish ones took their lamps but did not take along any extra oil.

verses.esv: but the wise took flasks of oil with their lamps.
verses.kjv: But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
verses.bsb: But the wise ones took oil in flasks along with their lamps.

verses.esv: As the bridegroom was delayed, they all became drowsy and slept.
verses.kjv: While the bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept.
verses.bsb: When the bridegroom was delayed, they all became drowsy and fell asleep.

verses.esv: But at midnight there was a cry, ‘Here is the bridegroom! Come out to meet him.’
verses.kjv: And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him.
verses.bsb: At midnight the cry rang out: ‘Here is the bridegroom! Come out to meet him!’

verses.esv: Then all those virgins rose and trimmed their lamps.
verses.kjv: Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.
verses.bsb: Then all the virgins woke up and trimmed their lamps.

verses.esv: And the foolish said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’
verses.kjv: And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are gone out.
verses.bsb: The foolish ones said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil; our lamps are going out.’

verses.esv: But the wise answered, saying, ‘Since there will not be enough for us and for you, go rather to the dealers and buy for yourselves.’
verses.kjv: But the wise answered, saying, Not so; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.
verses.bsb: ‘No,’ said the wise ones, ‘or there may not be enough for both us and you. Instead, go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.’

verses.esv: And while they were going to buy, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the marriage feast, and the door was shut.
verses.kjv: And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage: and the door was shut.
verses.bsb: But while they were on their way to buy it, the bridegroom arrived. Those who were ready went in with him to the wedding banquet, and the door was shut.

verses.esv: Afterward the other virgins came also, saying, ‘Lord, lord, open to us.’
verses.kjv: Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.
verses.bsb: Later the other virgins arrived and said, ‘Lord, lord, open the door for us!’

verses.esv: But he answered, ‘Truly, I say to you, I do not know you.’
verses.kjv: But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not.
verses.bsb: But he replied, ‘Truly I tell you, I do not know you.’

verses.esv: Watch therefore, for you know neither the day nor the hour.
verses.kjv: Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.
verses.bsb: Therefore keep watch, because you do not know the day or the hour.

verses.esv: “For it will be like a man going on a journey, who called his servants and entrusted to them his property.
verses.kjv: ¶ For the kingdom of heaven is as a man travelling into a far country, who called his own servants, and delivered unto them his goods.
verses.bsb: For it is just like a man going on a journey, who called his servants and entrusted them with his possessions.

verses.esv: To one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one, to each according to his ability. Then he went away.
verses.kjv: And unto one he gave five talents, to another two, and to another one; to every man according to his several ability; and straightway took his journey.
verses.bsb: To one he gave five talents, to another two talents, and to another one talent—each according to his own ability. And he went on his journey.

verses.esv: He who had received the five talents went at once and traded with them, and he made five talents more.
verses.kjv: Then he that had received the five talents went and traded with the same, and made them other five talents.
verses.bsb: The servant who had received the five talents went at once and put them to work and gained five more.

verses.esv: So also he who had the two talents made two talents more.
verses.kjv: And likewise he that had received two, he also gained other two.
verses.bsb: Likewise, the one with the two talents gained two more.

verses.esv: But he who had received the one talent went and dug in the ground and hid his master's money.
verses.kjv: But he that had received one went and digged in the earth, and hid his lord’s money.
verses.bsb: But the servant who had received the one talent went off, dug a hole in the ground, and hid his master’s money.

verses.esv: Now after a long time the master of those servants came and settled accounts with them.
verses.kjv: After a long time the lord of those servants cometh, and reckoneth with them.
verses.bsb: After a long time the master of those servants returned to settle accounts with them.

verses.esv: And he who had received the five talents came forward, bringing five talents more, saying, ‘Master, you delivered to me five talents; here, I have made five talents more.’
verses.kjv: And so he that had received five talents came and brought other five talents, saying, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents: behold, I have gained beside them five talents more.
verses.bsb: The servant who had received the five talents came and presented five more. ‘Master,’ he said, ‘you entrusted me with five talents. See, I have gained five more.’

verses.esv: His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a little; I will set you over much. Enter into the joy of your master.’
verses.kjv: His lord said unto him, Well done, thou good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.
verses.bsb: His master replied, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Enter into the joy of your master!’

verses.esv: And he also who had the two talents came forward, saying, ‘Master, you delivered to me two talents; here, I have made two talents more.’
verses.kjv: He also that had received two talents came and said, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me two talents: behold, I have gained two other talents beside them.
verses.bsb: The servant who had received the two talents also came and said, ‘Master, you entrusted me with two talents. See, I have gained two more.’

verses.esv: His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a little; I will set you over much. Enter into the joy of your master.’
verses.kjv: His lord said unto him, Well done, good and faithful servant; thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.
verses.bsb: His master replied, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Enter into the joy of your master!’

verses.esv: He also who had received the one talent came forward, saying, ‘Master, I knew you to be a hard man, reaping where you did not sow, and gathering where you scattered no seed,
verses.kjv: Then he which had received the one talent came and said, Lord, I knew thee that thou art an hard man, reaping where thou hast not sown, and gathering where thou hast not strawed:
verses.bsb: Finally, the servant who had received the one talent came and said, ‘Master, I knew that you are a hard man, reaping where you have not sown and gathering where you have not scattered seed.

verses.esv: so I was afraid, and I went and hid your talent in the ground. Here, you have what is yours.’
verses.kjv: And I was afraid, and went and hid thy talent in the earth: lo, there thou hast that is thine.
verses.bsb: So I was afraid and went out and hid your talent in the ground. See, you have what belongs to you.’

verses.esv: But his master answered him, ‘You wicked and slothful servant! You knew that I reap where I have not sown and gather where I scattered no seed?
verses.kjv: His lord answered and said unto him, Thou wicked and slothful servant, thou knewest that I reap where I sowed not, and gather where I have not strawed:
verses.bsb: ‘You wicked, lazy servant!’ replied his master. ‘You knew that I reap where I have not sown and gather where I have not scattered seed.

verses.esv: Then you ought to have invested my money with the bankers, and at my coming I should have received what was my own with interest.
verses.kjv: Thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the exchangers, and then at my coming I should have received mine own with usury.
verses.bsb: Then you should have deposited my money with the bankers, and on my return I would have received it back with interest.

verses.esv: So take the talent from him and give it to him who has the ten talents.
verses.kjv: Take therefore the talent from him, and give it unto him which hath ten talents.
verses.bsb: Therefore take the talent from him and give it to the one who has ten talents.

verses.esv: For to everyone who has will more be given, and he will have an abundance. But from the one who has not, even what he has will be taken away.
verses.kjv: For unto every one that hath shall be given, and he shall have abundance: but from him that hath not shall be taken away even that which he hath.
verses.bsb: For everyone who has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. But the one who does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.

verses.esv: And cast the worthless servant into the outer darkness. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
verses.kjv: And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
verses.bsb: And throw that worthless servant into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’

verses.esv: “When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, then he will sit on his glorious throne.
verses.kjv: ¶ When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then shall he sit upon the throne of his glory:
verses.bsb: When the Son of Man comes in His glory, and all the angels with Him, He will sit on His glorious throne.

verses.esv: Before him will be gathered all the nations, and he will separate people one from another as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
verses.kjv: And before him shall be gathered all nations: and he shall separate them one from another, as a shepherd divideth his sheep from the goats:
verses.bsb: All the nations will be gathered before Him, and He will separate the people one from another, as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.

verses.esv: And he will place the sheep on his right, but the goats on the left.
verses.kjv: And he shall set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.
verses.bsb: He will place the sheep on His right and the goats on His left.

verses.esv: Then the King will say to those on his right, ‘Come, you who are blessed by my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
verses.kjv: Then shall the King say unto them on his right hand, Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world:
verses.bsb: Then the King will say to those on His right, ‘Come, you who are blessed by My Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.

verses.esv: For I was hungry and you gave me food, I was thirsty and you gave me drink, I was a stranger and you welcomed me,
verses.kjv: For I was an hungred, and ye gave me meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me drink: I was a stranger, and ye took me in:
verses.bsb: For I was hungry and you gave Me something to eat, I was thirsty and you gave Me something to drink, I was a stranger and you took Me in,

verses.esv: I was naked and you clothed me, I was sick and you visited me, I was in prison and you came to me.’
verses.kjv: Naked, and ye clothed me: I was sick, and ye visited me: I was in prison, and ye came unto me.
verses.bsb: I was naked and you clothed Me, I was sick and you looked after Me, I was in prison and you visited Me.’

verses.esv: Then the righteous will answer him, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you drink?
verses.kjv: Then shall the righteous answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, and fed thee? or thirsty, and gave thee drink?
verses.bsb: Then the righteous will answer Him, ‘Lord, when did we see You hungry and feed You, or thirsty and give You something to drink?

verses.esv: And when did we see you a stranger and welcome you, or naked and clothe you?
verses.kjv: When saw we thee a stranger, and took thee in? or naked, and clothed thee?
verses.bsb: When did we see You a stranger and take You in, or naked and clothe You?

verses.esv: And when did we see you sick or in prison and visit you?’
verses.kjv: Or when saw we thee sick, or in prison, and came unto thee?
verses.bsb: When did we see You sick or in prison and visit You?’

verses.esv: And the King will answer them, ‘Truly, I say to you, as you did it to one of the least of these my brothers, you did it to me.’
verses.kjv: And the King shall answer and say unto them, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it unto me.
verses.bsb: And the King will reply, ‘Truly I tell you, whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers of Mine, you did for Me.’

verses.esv: “Then he will say to those on his left, ‘Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels.
verses.kjv: Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels:
verses.bsb: Then He will say to those on His left, ‘Depart from Me, you who are cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels.

verses.esv: For I was hungry and you gave me no food, I was thirsty and you gave me no drink,
verses.kjv: For I was an hungred, and ye gave me no meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me no drink:
verses.bsb: For I was hungry and you gave Me nothing to eat, I was thirsty and you gave Me nothing to drink,

verses.esv: I was a stranger and you did not welcome me, naked and you did not clothe me, sick and in prison and you did not visit me.’
verses.kjv: I was a stranger, and ye took me not in: naked, and ye clothed me not: sick, and in prison, and ye visited me not.
verses.bsb: I was a stranger and you did not take Me in, I was naked and you did not clothe Me, I was sick and in prison and you did not visit Me.’

verses.esv: Then they also will answer, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison, and did not minister to you?’
verses.kjv: Then shall they also answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, or athirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not minister unto thee?
verses.bsb: And they too will reply, ‘Lord, when did we see You hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison, and did not minister to You?’

verses.esv: Then he will answer them, saying, ‘Truly, I say to you, as you did not do it to one of the least of these, you did not do it to me.’
verses.kjv: Then shall he answer them, saying, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye did it not to one of the least of these, ye did it not to me.
verses.bsb: Then the King will answer, ‘Truly I tell you, whatever you did not do for one of the least of these, you did not do for Me.’

verses.esv: And these will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”
verses.kjv: And these shall go away into everlasting punishment: but the righteous into life eternal.
verses.bsb: And they will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”

verses.esv: When Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said to his disciples,
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, when Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said unto his disciples,
verses.bsb: When Jesus had finished saying all these things, He told His disciples,

verses.esv: “You know that after two days the Passover is coming, and the Son of Man will be delivered up to be crucified.”
verses.kjv: Ye know that after two days is the feast of the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.
verses.bsb: “You know that the Passover is two days away, and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified.”

verses.esv: Then the chief priests and the elders of the people gathered in the palace of the high priest, whose name was Caiaphas,
verses.kjv: Then assembled together the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders of the people, unto the palace of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas,
verses.bsb: At that time the chief priests and elders of the people assembled in the courtyard of the high priest, whose name was Caiaphas,

verses.esv: and plotted together in order to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill him.
verses.kjv: And consulted that they might take Jesus by subtilty, and kill him.
verses.bsb: and they conspired to arrest Jesus covertly and kill Him.

verses.esv: But they said, “Not during the feast, lest there be an uproar among the people.”
verses.kjv: But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar among the people.
verses.bsb: “But not during the feast,” they said, “or there may be a riot among the people.”

verses.esv: Now when Jesus was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper,
verses.kjv: ¶ Now when Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,
verses.bsb: While Jesus was in Bethany in the home of Simon the Leper,

verses.esv: a woman came up to him with an alabaster flask of very expensive ointment, and she poured it on his head as he reclined at table.
verses.kjv: There came unto him a woman having an alabaster box of very precious ointment, and poured it on his head, as he sat at meat.
verses.bsb: a woman came to Him with an alabaster jar of expensive perfume, which she poured on His head as He reclined at the table.

verses.esv: And when the disciples saw it, they were indignant, saying, “Why this waste?
verses.kjv: But when his disciples saw it, they had indignation, saying, To what purpose is this waste?
verses.bsb: When the disciples saw this, they were indignant and asked, “Why this waste?

verses.esv: For this could have been sold for a large sum and given to the poor.”
verses.kjv: For this ointment might have been sold for much, and given to the poor.
verses.bsb: This perfume could have been sold at a high price, and the money given to the poor.”

verses.esv: But Jesus, aware of this, said to them, “Why do you trouble the woman? For she has done a beautiful thing to me.
verses.kjv: When Jesus understood it, he said unto them, Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me.
verses.bsb: Aware of this, Jesus asked, “Why are you bothering this woman? She has done a beautiful deed to Me.

verses.esv: For you always have the poor with you, but you will not always have me.
verses.kjv: For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always.
verses.bsb: The poor you will always have with you, but you will not always have Me.

verses.esv: In pouring this ointment on my body, she has done it to prepare me for burial.
verses.kjv: For in that she hath poured this ointment on my body, she did it for my burial.
verses.bsb: By pouring this perfume on Me, she has prepared My body for burial.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, wherever this gospel is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will also be told in memory of her.”
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, wherever this gospel is preached in all the world, what she has done will also be told in memory of her.”

verses.esv: Then one of the twelve, whose name was Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests
verses.kjv: ¶ Then one of the twelve, called Judas Iscariot, went unto the chief priests,
verses.bsb: Then one of the Twelve, the one called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests

verses.esv: and said, “What will you give me if I deliver him over to you?” And they paid him thirty pieces of silver.
verses.kjv: And said unto them, What will ye give me, and I will deliver him unto you? And they covenanted with him for thirty pieces of silver.
verses.bsb: and asked, “What are you willing to give me if I hand Him over to you?” And they set out for him thirty pieces of silver.

verses.esv: And from that moment he sought an opportunity to betray him.
verses.kjv: And from that time he sought opportunity to betray him.
verses.bsb: So from then on Judas looked for an opportunity to betray Jesus.

verses.esv: Now on the first day of Unleavened Bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying, “Where will you have us prepare for you to eat the Passover?”
verses.kjv: ¶ Now the first day of the feast of unleavened bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the passover?
verses.bsb: On the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Where do You want us to prepare for You to eat the Passover?”

verses.esv: He said, “Go into the city to a certain man and say to him, ‘The Teacher says, My time is at hand. I will keep the Passover at your house with my disciples.’”
verses.kjv: And he said, Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand; I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples.
verses.bsb: He answered, “Go into the city to a certain man and tell him that the Teacher says, ‘My time is near. I will keep the Passover with My disciples at your house.’”

verses.esv: And the disciples did as Jesus had directed them, and they prepared the Passover.
verses.kjv: And the disciples did as Jesus had appointed them; and they made ready the passover.
verses.bsb: So the disciples did as Jesus had directed them and prepared the Passover.

verses.esv: When it was evening, he reclined at table with the twelve.
verses.kjv: Now when the even was come, he sat down with the twelve.
verses.bsb: When evening came, Jesus was reclining with the twelve disciples.

verses.esv: And as they were eating, he said, “Truly, I say to you, one of you will betray me.”
verses.kjv: And as they did eat, he said, Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
verses.bsb: And while they were eating, He said to them, “Truly I tell you, one of you will betray Me.”

verses.esv: And they were very sorrowful and began to say to him one after another, “Is it I, Lord?”
verses.kjv: And they were exceeding sorrowful, and began every one of them to say unto him, Lord, is it I?
verses.bsb: They were deeply grieved and began to ask Him one after another, “Surely not I, Lord?”

verses.esv: He answered, “He who has dipped his hand in the dish with me will betray me.
verses.kjv: And he answered and said, He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “The one who has dipped his hand into the bowl with Me will betray Me.

verses.esv: The Son of Man goes as it is written of him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that man if he had not been born.”
verses.kjv: The Son of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe unto that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! it had been good for that man if he had not been born.
verses.bsb: The Son of Man will go just as it is written about Him, but woe to that man by whom He is betrayed. It would be better for him if he had not been born.”

verses.esv: Judas, who would betray him, answered, “Is it I, Rabbi?” He said to him, “You have said so.”
verses.kjv: Then Judas, which betrayed him, answered and said, Master, is it I? He said unto him, Thou hast said.
verses.bsb: Then Judas, who would betray Him, said, “Surely not I, Rabbi?”  Jesus answered, “You have said it yourself.”

verses.esv: Now as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and after blessing it broke it and gave it to the disciples, and said, “Take, eat; this is my body.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and blessed it, and brake it, and gave it to the disciples, and said, Take, eat; this is my body.
verses.bsb: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, spoke a blessing and broke it, and gave it to the disciples, saying, “Take and eat; this is My body.”

verses.esv: And he took a cup, and when he had given thanks he gave it to them, saying, “Drink of it, all of you,
verses.kjv: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink ye all of it;
verses.bsb: Then He took the cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, “Drink from it, all of you.

verses.esv: for this is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.
verses.kjv: For this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many for the remission of sins.
verses.bsb: This is My blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.

verses.esv: I tell you I will not drink again of this fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father's kingdom.”
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father’s kingdom.
verses.bsb: I tell you, I will not drink of this fruit of the vine from now on until that day when I drink it anew with you in My Father’s kingdom.”

verses.esv: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
verses.kjv: And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.
verses.bsb: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

verses.esv: Then Jesus said to them, “You will all fall away because of me this night. For it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’
verses.kjv: Then saith Jesus unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to them, “This very night you will all fall away on account of Me. For it is written:  ‘I will strike the Shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’

verses.esv: But after I am raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.”
verses.kjv: But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.
verses.bsb: But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you into Galilee.”

verses.esv: Peter answered him, “Though they all fall away because of you, I will never fall away.”
verses.kjv: Peter answered and said unto him, Though all men shall be offended because of thee, yet will I never be offended.
verses.bsb: Peter said to Him, “Even if all fall away on account of You, I never will.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “Truly, I tell you, this very night, before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times.”
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
verses.bsb: “Truly I tell you,” Jesus declared, “this very night, before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times.”

verses.esv: Peter said to him, “Even if I must die with you, I will not deny you!” And all the disciples said the same.
verses.kjv: Peter said unto him, Though I should die with thee, yet will I not deny thee. Likewise also said all the disciples.
verses.bsb: Peter replied, “Even if I have to die with You, I will never deny You.” And all the other disciples said the same thing.

verses.esv: Then Jesus went with them to a place called Gethsemane, and he said to his disciples, “Sit here, while I go over there and pray.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane, and saith unto the disciples, Sit ye here, while I go and pray yonder.
verses.bsb: At that time Jesus went with His disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and He told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”

verses.esv: And taking with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, he began to be sorrowful and troubled.
verses.kjv: And he took with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to be sorrowful and very heavy.
verses.bsb: He took with Him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee and began to be sorrowful and deeply distressed.

verses.esv: Then he said to them, “My soul is very sorrowful, even to death; remain here, and watch with me.”
verses.kjv: Then saith he unto them, My soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death: tarry ye here, and watch with me.
verses.bsb: Then He said to them, “My soul is consumed with sorrow to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch with Me.”

verses.esv: And going a little farther he fell on his face and prayed, saying, “My Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me; nevertheless, not as I will, but as you will.”
verses.kjv: And he went a little further, and fell on his face, and prayed, saying, O my Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me: nevertheless not as I will, but as thou wilt.
verses.bsb: Going a little farther, He fell facedown and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from Me. Yet not as I will, but as You will.”

verses.esv: And he came to the disciples and found them sleeping. And he said to Peter, “So, could you not watch with me one hour?
verses.kjv: And he cometh unto the disciples, and findeth them asleep, and saith unto Peter, What, could ye not watch with me one hour?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus returned to the disciples and found them sleeping. “Were you not able to keep watch with Me for one hour?” He asked Peter.

verses.esv: Watch and pray that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
verses.kjv: Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation: the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
verses.bsb: “Watch and pray so that you will not enter into temptation. For the spirit is willing, but the body is weak.”

verses.esv: Again, for the second time, he went away and prayed, “My Father, if this cannot pass unless I drink it, your will be done.”
verses.kjv: He went away again the second time, and prayed, saying, O my Father, if this cup may not pass away from me, except I drink it, thy will be done.
verses.bsb: A second time He went away and prayed, “My Father, if this cup cannot pass unless I drink it, may Your will be done.”

verses.esv: And again he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.
verses.kjv: And he came and found them asleep again: for their eyes were heavy.
verses.bsb: And again Jesus returned and found them sleeping—for their eyes were heavy.

verses.esv: So, leaving them again, he went away and prayed for the third time, saying the same words again.
verses.kjv: And he left them, and went away again, and prayed the third time, saying the same words.
verses.bsb: So He left them and went away once more and prayed a third time, saying the same thing.

verses.esv: Then he came to the disciples and said to them, “Sleep and take your rest later on. See, the hour is at hand, and the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
verses.kjv: Then cometh he to his disciples, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take your rest: behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
verses.bsb: Then He returned to the disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Look, the hour is near, and the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

verses.esv: Rise, let us be going; see, my betrayer is at hand.”
verses.kjv: Rise, let us be going: behold, he is at hand that doth betray me.
verses.bsb: Rise, let us go! See, My betrayer is approaching!”

verses.esv: While he was still speaking, Judas came, one of the twelve, and with him a great crowd with swords and clubs, from the chief priests and the elders of the people.
verses.kjv: ¶ And while he yet spake, lo, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and elders of the people.
verses.bsb: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, arrived, accompanied by a large crowd armed with swords and clubs, sent from the chief priests and elders of the people.

verses.esv: Now the betrayer had given them a sign, saying, “The one I will kiss is the man; seize him.”
verses.kjv: Now he that betrayed him gave them a sign, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is he: hold him fast.
verses.bsb: Now the betrayer had arranged a signal with them: “The One I kiss is the man; arrest Him.”

verses.esv: And he came up to Jesus at once and said, “Greetings, Rabbi!” And he kissed him.
verses.kjv: And forthwith he came to Jesus, and said, Hail, master; and kissed him.
verses.bsb: Going directly to Jesus, he said, “Greetings, Rabbi!” and kissed Him.

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “Friend, do what you came to do.” Then they came up and laid hands on Jesus and seized him.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Friend, wherefore art thou come? Then came they, and laid hands on Jesus, and took him.
verses.bsb: “Friend,” Jesus replied, “do what you came for.”  Then the men stepped forward, seized Jesus, and arrested Him.

verses.esv: And behold, one of those who were with Jesus stretched out his hand and drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
verses.kjv: And, behold, one of them which were with Jesus stretched out his hand, and drew his sword, and struck a servant of the high priest’s, and smote off his ear.
verses.bsb: At this, one of Jesus’ companions drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his ear.

verses.esv: Then Jesus said to him, “Put your sword back into its place. For all who take the sword will perish by the sword.
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto him, Put up again thy sword into his place: for all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword.
verses.bsb: “Put your sword back in its place,” Jesus said to him. “For all who draw the sword will die by the sword.

verses.esv: Do you think that I cannot appeal to my Father, and he will at once send me more than twelve legions of angels?
verses.kjv: Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my Father, and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels?
verses.bsb: Are you not aware that I can call on My Father, and He will at once put at My disposal more than twelve legions of angels?

verses.esv: But how then should the Scriptures be fulfilled, that it must be so?”
verses.kjv: But how then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be?
verses.bsb: But how then would the Scriptures be fulfilled that say it must happen this way?”

verses.esv: At that hour Jesus said to the crowds, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs to capture me? Day after day I sat in the temple teaching, and you did not seize me.
verses.kjv: In that same hour said Jesus to the multitudes, Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me? I sat daily with you teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me.
verses.bsb: At that time Jesus said to the crowd, “Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest Me as you would an outlaw? Every day I sat teaching in the temple courts, and you did not arrest Me.

verses.esv: But all this has taken place that the Scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.” Then all the disciples left him and fled.
verses.kjv: But all this was done, that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples forsook him, and fled.
verses.bsb: But this has all happened so that the writings of the prophets would be fulfilled.”  Then all the disciples deserted Him and fled.

verses.esv: Then those who had seized Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders had gathered.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they that had laid hold on Jesus led him away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were assembled.
verses.bsb: Those who had arrested Jesus led Him away to the house of Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and elders had gathered.

verses.esv: And Peter was following him at a distance, as far as the courtyard of the high priest, and going inside he sat with the guards to see the end.
verses.kjv: But Peter followed him afar off unto the high priest’s palace, and went in, and sat with the servants, to see the end.
verses.bsb: But Peter followed Him at a distance, right up to the courtyard of the high priest. And he went in and sat down with the guards to see the outcome.

verses.esv: Now the chief priests and the whole council were seeking false testimony against Jesus that they might put him to death,
verses.kjv: Now the chief priests, and elders, and all the council, sought false witness against Jesus, to put him to death;
verses.bsb: Now the chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were seeking false testimony against Jesus in order to put Him to death.

verses.esv: but they found none, though many false witnesses came forward. At last two came forward
verses.kjv: But found none: yea, though many false witnesses came, yet found they none. At the last came two false witnesses,
verses.bsb: But they did not find any, though many false witnesses came forward.  Finally two came forward

verses.esv: and said, “This man said, ‘I am able to destroy the temple of God, and to rebuild it in three days.’”
verses.kjv: And said, This fellow said, I am able to destroy the temple of God, and to build it in three days.
verses.bsb: and declared, “This man said, ‘I am able to destroy the temple of God and rebuild it in three days.’”

verses.esv: And the high priest stood up and said, “Have you no answer to make? What is it that these men testify against you?”
verses.kjv: And the high priest arose, and said unto him, Answerest thou nothing? what is it which these witness against thee?
verses.bsb: So the high priest stood up and asked Him, “Have You no answer? What are these men testifying against You?”

verses.esv: But Jesus remained silent. And the high priest said to him, “I adjure you by the living God, tell us if you are the Christ, the Son of God.”
verses.kjv: But Jesus held his peace. And the high priest answered and said unto him, I adjure thee by the living God, that thou tell us whether thou be the Christ, the Son of God.
verses.bsb: But Jesus remained silent.  Then the high priest said to Him, “I charge You under oath by the living God: Tell us if You are the Christ, the Son of God.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to him, “You have said so. But I tell you, from now on you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven.”
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Thou hast said: nevertheless I say unto you, Hereafter shall ye see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.
verses.bsb: “You have said it yourself,” Jesus answered. “But I say to all of you, from now on you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven.”

verses.esv: Then the high priest tore his robes and said, “He has uttered blasphemy. What further witnesses do we need? You have now heard his blasphemy.
verses.kjv: Then the high priest rent his clothes, saying, He hath spoken blasphemy; what further need have we of witnesses? behold, now ye have heard his blasphemy.
verses.bsb: At this, the high priest tore his clothes and declared, “He has blasphemed! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, now you have heard the blasphemy.

verses.esv: What is your judgment?” They answered, “He deserves death.”
verses.kjv: What think ye? They answered and said, He is guilty of death.
verses.bsb: What do you think?”  “He deserves to die,” they answered.

verses.esv: Then they spit in his face and struck him. And some slapped him,
verses.kjv: Then did they spit in his face, and buffeted him; and others smote him with the palms of their hands,
verses.bsb: Then they spit in His face and struck Him. Others slapped Him

verses.esv: saying, “Prophesy to us, you Christ! Who is it that struck you?”
verses.kjv: Saying, Prophesy unto us, thou Christ, Who is he that smote thee?
verses.bsb: and said, “Prophesy to us, Christ! Who hit You?”

verses.esv: Now Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. And a servant girl came up to him and said, “You also were with Jesus the Galilean.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Now Peter sat without in the palace: and a damsel came unto him, saying, Thou also wast with Jesus of Galilee.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, and a servant girl came up to him. “You also were with Jesus the Galilean,” she said.

verses.esv: But he denied it before them all, saying, “I do not know what you mean.”
verses.kjv: But he denied before them all, saying, I know not what thou sayest.
verses.bsb: But he denied it before them all: “I do not know what you are talking about.”

verses.esv: And when he went out to the entrance, another servant girl saw him, and she said to the bystanders, “This man was with Jesus of Nazareth.”
verses.kjv: And when he was gone out into the porch, another maid saw him, and said unto them that were there, This fellow was also with Jesus of Nazareth.
verses.bsb: When Peter had gone out to the gateway, another servant girl saw him and said to the people there, “This man was with Jesus of Nazareth.”

verses.esv: And again he denied it with an oath: “I do not know the man.”
verses.kjv: And again he denied with an oath, I do not know the man.
verses.bsb: And again he denied it with an oath: “I do not know the man!”

verses.esv: After a little while the bystanders came up and said to Peter, “Certainly you too are one of them, for your accent betrays you.”
verses.kjv: And after a while came unto him they that stood by, and said to Peter, Surely thou also art one of them; for thy speech bewrayeth thee.
verses.bsb: After a little while, those standing nearby came up to Peter. “Surely you are one of them,” they said, “for your accent gives you away.”

verses.esv: Then he began to invoke a curse on himself and to swear, “I do not know the man.” And immediately the rooster crowed.
verses.kjv: Then began he to curse and to swear, saying, I know not the man. And immediately the cock crew.
verses.bsb: At that he began to curse and swear to them, “I do not know the man!”  And immediately a rooster crowed.

verses.esv: And Peter remembered the saying of Jesus, “Before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times.” And he went out and wept bitterly.
verses.kjv: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus, which said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice. And he went out, and wept bitterly.
verses.bsb: Then Peter remembered the word that Jesus had spoken: “Before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times.” And he went outside and wept bitterly.

verses.esv: When morning came, all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death.
verses.kjv: WHEN the morning was come, all the chief priests and elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death:
verses.bsb: When morning came, all the chief priests and elders of the people conspired against Jesus to put Him to death.

verses.esv: And they bound him and led him away and delivered him over to Pilate the governor.
verses.kjv: And when they had bound him, they led him away, and delivered him to Pontius Pilate the governor.
verses.bsb: They bound Him, led Him away, and handed Him over to Pilate the governor.

verses.esv: Then when Judas, his betrayer, saw that Jesus was condemned, he changed his mind and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and the elders,
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Judas, which had betrayed him, when he saw that he was condemned, repented himself, and brought again the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
verses.bsb: When Judas, who had betrayed Him, saw that Jesus was condemned, he was filled with remorse and returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders.

verses.esv: saying, “I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.” They said, “What is that to us? See to it yourself.”
verses.kjv: Saying, I have sinned in that I have betrayed the innocent blood. And they said, What is that to us? see thou to that.
verses.bsb: “I have sinned by betraying innocent blood,” he said.  “What is that to us?” they replied. “You bear the responsibility.”

verses.esv: And throwing down the pieces of silver into the temple, he departed, and he went and hanged himself.
verses.kjv: And he cast down the pieces of silver in the temple, and departed, and went and hanged himself.
verses.bsb: So Judas threw the silver into the temple and left. Then he went away and hanged himself.

verses.esv: But the chief priests, taking the pieces of silver, said, “It is not lawful to put them into the treasury, since it is blood money.”
verses.kjv: And the chief priests took the silver pieces, and said, It is not lawful for to put them into the treasury, because it is the price of blood.
verses.bsb: The chief priests picked up the pieces of silver and said, “It is unlawful to put this into the treasury, since it is blood money.”

verses.esv: So they took counsel and bought with them the potter's field as a burial place for strangers.
verses.kjv: And they took counsel, and bought with them the potter’s field, to bury strangers in.
verses.bsb: After conferring together, they used the money to buy the potter’s field as a burial place for foreigners.

verses.esv: Therefore that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
verses.kjv: Wherefore that field was called, The field of blood, unto this day.
verses.bsb: That is why it has been called the Field of Blood to this day.

verses.esv: Then was fulfilled what had been spoken by the prophet Jeremiah, saying, “And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him on whom a price had been set by some of the sons of Israel,
verses.kjv: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremy the prophet, saying, And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him that was valued, whom they of the children of Israel did value;
verses.bsb: Then what was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled:  “They took the thirty pieces of silver, the price set on Him by the people of Israel,

verses.esv: and they gave them for the potter's field, as the Lord directed me.”
verses.kjv: And gave them for the potter’s field, as the Lord appointed me.
verses.bsb: and they gave them for the potter’s field, as the Lord had commanded me.”

verses.esv: Now Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” Jesus said, “You have said so.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus stood before the governor: and the governor asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And Jesus said unto him, Thou sayest.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile Jesus stood before the governor, who questioned Him: “Are You the King of the Jews?”  “You have said so,” Jesus replied.

verses.esv: But when he was accused by the chief priests and elders, he gave no answer.
verses.kjv: And when he was accused of the chief priests and elders, he answered nothing.
verses.bsb: And when He was accused by the chief priests and elders, He gave no answer.

verses.esv: Then Pilate said to him, “Do you not hear how many things they testify against you?”
verses.kjv: Then said Pilate unto him, Hearest thou not how many things they witness against thee?
verses.bsb: Then Pilate asked Him, “Do You not hear how many charges they are bringing against You?”

verses.esv: But he gave him no answer, not even to a single charge, so that the governor was greatly amazed.
verses.kjv: And he answered him to never a word; insomuch that the governor marvelled greatly.
verses.bsb: But Jesus gave no answer, not even to a single charge, much to the governor’s amazement.

verses.esv: Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to release for the crowd any one prisoner whom they wanted.
verses.kjv: Now at that feast the governor was wont to release unto the people a prisoner, whom they would.
verses.bsb: Now it was the governor’s custom at the feast to release to the crowd a prisoner of their choosing.

verses.esv: And they had then a notorious prisoner called Barabbas.
verses.kjv: And they had then a notable prisoner, called Barabbas.
verses.bsb: At that time they were holding a notorious prisoner named Barabbas.

verses.esv: So when they had gathered, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release for you: Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?”
verses.kjv: Therefore when they were gathered together, Pilate said unto them, Whom will ye that I release unto you? Barabbas, or Jesus which is called Christ?
verses.bsb: So when the crowd had assembled, Pilate asked them, “Which one do you want me to release to you: Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?”

verses.esv: For he knew that it was out of envy that they had delivered him up.
verses.kjv: For he knew that for envy they had delivered him.
verses.bsb: For he knew it was out of envy that they had handed Jesus over to him.

verses.esv: Besides, while he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent word to him, “Have nothing to do with that righteous man, for I have suffered much because of him today in a dream.”
verses.kjv: ¶ When he was set down on the judgment seat, his wife sent unto him, saying, Have thou nothing to do with that just man: for I have suffered many things this day in a dream because of him.
verses.bsb: While Pilate was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent him this message: “Have nothing to do with that innocent man, for I have suffered terribly in a dream today because of Him.”

verses.esv: Now the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowd to ask for Barabbas and destroy Jesus.
verses.kjv: But the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitude that they should ask Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.
verses.bsb: But the chief priests and elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and to have Jesus put to death.

verses.esv: The governor again said to them, “Which of the two do you want me to release for you?” And they said, “Barabbas.”
verses.kjv: The governor answered and said unto them, Whether of the twain will ye that I release unto you? They said, Barabbas.
verses.bsb: “Which of the two do you want me to release to you?” asked the governor.  “Barabbas,” they replied.

verses.esv: Pilate said to them, “Then what shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?” They all said, “Let him be crucified!”
verses.kjv: Pilate saith unto them, What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ? They all say unto him, Let him be crucified.
verses.bsb: “What then should I do with Jesus who is called Christ?” Pilate asked.  They all answered, “Crucify Him!”

verses.esv: And he said, “Why? What evil has he done?” But they shouted all the more, “Let him be crucified!”
verses.kjv: And the governor said, Why, what evil hath he done? But they cried out the more, saying, Let him be crucified.
verses.bsb: “Why?” asked Pilate. “What evil has He done?”  But they shouted all the louder, “Crucify Him!”

verses.esv: So when Pilate saw that he was gaining nothing, but rather that a riot was beginning, he took water and washed his hands before the crowd, saying, “I am innocent of this man's blood; see to it yourselves.”
verses.kjv: ¶ When Pilate saw that he could prevail nothing, but that rather a tumult was made, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, I am innocent of the blood of this just person: see ye to it.
verses.bsb: When Pilate saw that he was accomplishing nothing, but that instead a riot was breaking out, he took water and washed his hands before the crowd. “I am innocent of this man’s blood,” he said. “You bear the responsibility.”

verses.esv: And all the people answered, “His blood be on us and on our children!”
verses.kjv: Then answered all the people, and said, His blood be on us, and on our children.
verses.bsb: All the people answered, “His blood be on us and on our children!”

verses.esv: Then he released for them Barabbas, and having scourged Jesus, delivered him to be crucified.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then released he Barabbas unto them: and when he had scourged Jesus, he delivered him to be crucified.
verses.bsb: So Pilate released Barabbas to them. But he had Jesus flogged, and handed Him over to be crucified.

verses.esv: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the governor's headquarters, and they gathered the whole battalion before him.
verses.kjv: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the common hall, and gathered unto him the whole band of soldiers.
verses.bsb: Then the governor’s soldiers took Jesus into the Praetorium and gathered the whole company around Him.

verses.esv: And they stripped him and put a scarlet robe on him,
verses.kjv: And they stripped him, and put on him a scarlet robe.
verses.bsb: They stripped Him and put a scarlet robe on Him.

verses.esv: and twisting together a crown of thorns, they put it on his head and put a reed in his right hand. And kneeling before him, they mocked him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
verses.kjv: ¶ And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon his head, and a reed in his right hand: and they bowed the knee before him, and mocked him, saying, Hail, King of the Jews!
verses.bsb: And they twisted together a crown of thorns and set it on His head. They put a staff in His right hand and knelt down before Him to mock Him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”

verses.esv: And they spit on him and took the reed and struck him on the head.
verses.kjv: And they spit upon him, and took the reed, and smote him on the head.
verses.bsb: Then they spit on Him and took the staff and struck Him on the head repeatedly.

verses.esv: And when they had mocked him, they stripped him of the robe and put his own clothes on him and led him away to crucify him.
verses.kjv: And after that they had mocked him, they took the robe off from him, and put his own raiment on him, and led him away to crucify him.
verses.bsb: After they had mocked Him, they removed the robe and put His own clothes back on Him. Then they led Him away to crucify Him.

verses.esv: As they went out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name. They compelled this man to carry his cross.
verses.kjv: And as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name: him they compelled to bear his cross.
verses.bsb: Along the way they found a man from Cyrene, named Simon, and they forced him to carry the cross of Jesus.

verses.esv: And when they came to a place called Golgotha (which means Place of a Skull),
verses.kjv: And when they were come unto a place called Golgotha, that is to say, a place of a skull,
verses.bsb: And when they came to a place called Golgotha, which means The Place of the Skull,

verses.esv: they offered him wine to drink, mixed with gall, but when he tasted it, he would not drink it.
verses.kjv: ¶ They gave him vinegar to drink mingled with gall: and when he had tasted thereof, he would not drink.
verses.bsb: they offered Him wine to drink, mixed with gall; but after tasting it, He refused to drink it.

verses.esv: And when they had crucified him, they divided his garments among them by casting lots.
verses.kjv: And they crucified him, and parted his garments, casting lots: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, They parted my garments among them, and upon my vesture did they cast lots.
verses.bsb: When they had crucified Him, they divided up His garments by casting lots.

verses.esv: Then they sat down and kept watch over him there.
verses.kjv: And sitting down they watched him there;
verses.bsb: And sitting down, they kept watch over Him there.

verses.esv: And over his head they put the charge against him, which read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
verses.kjv: And set up over his head his accusation written, THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
verses.bsb: Above His head they posted the written charge against Him:  THIS IS JESUS,  THE KING OF THE JEWS.

verses.esv: Then two robbers were crucified with him, one on the right and one on the left.
verses.kjv: Then were there two thieves crucified with him, one on the right hand, and another on the left.
verses.bsb: Two robbers were crucified with Him, one on His right hand and the other on His left.

verses.esv: And those who passed by derided him, wagging their heads
verses.kjv: ¶ And they that passed by reviled him, wagging their heads,
verses.bsb: And those who passed by heaped abuse on Him, shaking their heads

verses.esv: and saying, “You who would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save yourself! If you are the Son of God, come down from the cross.”
verses.kjv: And saying, Thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days, save thyself. If thou be the Son of God, come down from the cross.
verses.bsb: and saying, “You who are going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save Yourself! If You are the Son of God, come down from the cross!”

verses.esv: So also the chief priests, with the scribes and elders, mocked him, saying,
verses.kjv: Likewise also the chief priests mocking him, with the scribes and elders, said,
verses.bsb: In the same way, the chief priests, scribes, and elders mocked Him, saying,

verses.esv: “He saved others; he cannot save himself. He is the King of Israel; let him come down now from the cross, and we will believe in him.
verses.kjv: He saved others; himself he cannot save. If he be the King of Israel, let him now come down from the cross, and we will believe him.
verses.bsb: “He saved others, but He cannot save Himself. He is the King of Israel! Let Him come down now from the cross, and we will believe in Him.

verses.esv: He trusts in God; let God deliver him now, if he desires him. For he said, ‘I am the Son of God.’”
verses.kjv: He trusted in God; let him deliver him now, if he will have him: for he said, I am the Son of God.
verses.bsb: He trusts in God. Let God deliver Him now if He wants Him. For He said, ‘I am the Son of God.’”

verses.esv: And the robbers who were crucified with him also reviled him in the same way.
verses.kjv: The thieves also, which were crucified with him, cast the same in his teeth.
verses.bsb: In the same way, even the robbers who were crucified with Him berated Him.

verses.esv: Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land until the ninth hour.
verses.kjv: Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land unto the ninth hour.
verses.bsb: From the sixth hour until the ninth hour darkness came over all the land.

verses.esv: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice, saying, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” that is, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
verses.kjv: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani? that is to say, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
verses.bsb: About the ninth hour Jesus cried out in a loud voice, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me?”

verses.esv: And some of the bystanders, hearing it, said, “This man is calling Elijah.”
verses.kjv: Some of them that stood there, when they heard that, said, This man calleth for Elias.
verses.bsb: When some of those standing there heard this, they said, “He is calling Elijah.”

verses.esv: And one of them at once ran and took a sponge, filled it with sour wine, and put it on a reed and gave it to him to drink.
verses.kjv: And straightway one of them ran, and took a spunge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink.
verses.bsb: One of them quickly ran and brought a sponge. He filled it with sour wine, put it on a reed, and held it up for Jesus to drink.

verses.esv: But the others said, “Wait, let us see whether Elijah will come to save him.”
verses.kjv: The rest said, Let be, let us see whether Elias will come to save him.
verses.bsb: But the others said, “Leave Him alone. Let us see if Elijah comes to save Him.”

verses.esv: And Jesus cried out again with a loud voice and yielded up his spirit.
verses.kjv: ¶ Jesus, when he had cried again with a loud voice, yielded up the ghost.
verses.bsb: When Jesus had cried out again in a loud voice, He yielded up His spirit.

verses.esv: And behold, the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom. And the earth shook, and the rocks were split.
verses.kjv: And, behold, the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom; and the earth did quake, and the rocks rent;
verses.bsb: At that moment the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth quaked and the rocks were split.

verses.esv: The tombs also were opened. And many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised,
verses.kjv: And the graves were opened; and many bodies of the saints which slept arose,
verses.bsb: The tombs broke open, and the bodies of many saints who had fallen asleep were raised.

verses.esv: and coming out of the tombs after his resurrection they went into the holy city and appeared to many.
verses.kjv: And came out of the graves after his resurrection, and went into the holy city, and appeared unto many.
verses.bsb: After Jesus’ resurrection, when they had come out of the tombs, they entered the holy city and appeared to many people.

verses.esv: When the centurion and those who were with him, keeping watch over Jesus, saw the earthquake and what took place, they were filled with awe and said, “Truly this was the Son of God!”
verses.kjv: Now when the centurion, and they that were with him, watching Jesus, saw the earthquake, and those things that were done, they feared greatly, saying, Truly this was the Son of God.
verses.bsb: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and all that had happened, they were terrified and said, “Truly this was the Son of God.”

verses.esv: There were also many women there, looking on from a distance, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering to him,
verses.kjv: And many women were there beholding afar off, which followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him:
verses.bsb: And many women were there, watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to minister to Him.

verses.esv: among whom were Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joseph and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
verses.kjv: Among which was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee’s children.
verses.bsb: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedee’s sons.

verses.esv: When it was evening, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who also was a disciple of Jesus.
verses.kjv: When the even was come, there came a rich man of Arimathæa, named Joseph, who also himself was Jesus’ disciple:
verses.bsb: When it was evening, there came a rich man from Arimathea named Joseph, who himself was a disciple of Jesus.

verses.esv: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate ordered it to be given to him.
verses.kjv: He went to Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be delivered.
verses.bsb: He went to Pilate to ask for the body of Jesus, and Pilate ordered that it be given to him.

verses.esv: And Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen shroud
verses.kjv: And when Joseph had taken the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
verses.bsb: So Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,

verses.esv: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had cut in the rock. And he rolled a great stone to the entrance of the tomb and went away.
verses.kjv: And laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock: and he rolled a great stone to the door of the sepulchre, and departed.
verses.bsb: and placed it in his own new tomb that he had cut into the rock. Then he rolled a great stone across the entrance to the tomb and went away.

verses.esv: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting opposite the tomb.
verses.kjv: And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.
verses.bsb: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting there opposite the tomb.

verses.esv: The next day, that is, after the day of Preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate
verses.kjv: ¶ Now the next day, that followed the day of the preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate,
verses.bsb: The next day, the one after Preparation Day, the chief priests and Pharisees assembled before Pilate.

verses.esv: and said, “Sir, we remember how that impostor said, while he was still alive, ‘After three days I will rise.’
verses.kjv: Saying, Sir, we remember that that deceiver said, while he was yet alive, After three days I will rise again.
verses.bsb: “Sir,” they said, “we remember that while He was alive that deceiver said, ‘After three days I will rise again.’

verses.esv: Therefore order the tomb to be made secure until the third day, lest his disciples go and steal him away and tell the people, ‘He has risen from the dead,’ and the last fraud will be worse than the first.”
verses.kjv: Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest his disciples come by night, and steal him away, and say unto the people, He is risen from the dead: so the last error shall be worse than the first.
verses.bsb: So give the order that the tomb be secured until the third day. Otherwise, His disciples may come and steal Him away and tell the people He has risen from the dead. And this last deception would be worse than the first.”

verses.esv: Pilate said to them, “You have a guard of soldiers. Go, make it as secure as you can.”
verses.kjv: Pilate said unto them, Ye have a watch: go your way, make it as sure as ye can.
verses.bsb: “You have a guard,” Pilate said. “Go, make the tomb as secure as you know how.”

verses.esv: So they went and made the tomb secure by sealing the stone and setting a guard.
verses.kjv: So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.
verses.bsb: So they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.

verses.esv: Now after the Sabbath, toward the dawn of the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.
verses.kjv: IN the end of the sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.
verses.bsb: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.

verses.esv: And behold, there was a great earthquake, for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven and came and rolled back the stone and sat on it.
verses.kjv: And, behold, there was a great earthquake: for the angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat upon it.
verses.bsb: Suddenly there was a great earthquake, for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven, rolled away the stone, and sat on it.

verses.esv: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.
verses.kjv: His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow:
verses.bsb: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.

verses.esv: And for fear of him the guards trembled and became like dead men.
verses.kjv: And for fear of him the keepers did shake, and became as dead men.
verses.bsb: The guards trembled in fear of him and became like dead men.

verses.esv: But the angel said to the women, “Do not be afraid, for I know that you seek Jesus who was crucified.
verses.kjv: And the angel answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye: for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified.
verses.bsb: But the angel said to the women, “Do not be afraid, for I know that you are looking for Jesus, who was crucified.

verses.esv: He is not here, for he has risen, as he said. Come, see the place where he lay.
verses.kjv: He is not here: for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.
verses.bsb: He is not here; He has risen, just as He said! Come, see the place where He lay.

verses.esv: Then go quickly and tell his disciples that he has risen from the dead, and behold, he is going before you to Galilee; there you will see him. See, I have told you.”
verses.kjv: And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.
verses.bsb: Then go quickly and tell His disciples, ‘He has risen from the dead and is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see Him.’ See, I have told you.”

verses.esv: So they departed quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
verses.kjv: And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy; and did run to bring his disciples word.
verses.bsb: So they hurried away from the tomb in fear and great joy, and ran to tell His disciples.

verses.esv: And behold, Jesus met them and said, “Greetings!” And they came up and took hold of his feet and worshiped him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, All hail. And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.
verses.bsb: Suddenly Jesus met them and said, “Greetings!” They came to Him, grasped His feet, and worshiped Him.

verses.esv: Then Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid; go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee, and there they will see me.”
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.
verses.bsb: “Do not be afraid,” said Jesus. “Go and tell My brothers to go to Galilee. There they will see Me.”

verses.esv: While they were going, behold, some of the guard went into the city and told the chief priests all that had taken place.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the city, and shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done.
verses.bsb: While the women were on their way, some of the guards went into the city and reported to the chief priests all that had happened.

verses.esv: And when they had assembled with the elders and taken counsel, they gave a sufficient sum of money to the soldiers
verses.kjv: And when they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave large money unto the soldiers,
verses.bsb: And after the chief priests had met with the elders and formed a plan, they gave the soldiers a large sum of money

verses.esv: and said, “Tell people, ‘His disciples came by night and stole him away while we were asleep.’
verses.kjv: Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.
verses.bsb: and instructed them: “You are to say, ‘His disciples came by night and stole Him away while we were asleep.’

verses.esv: And if this comes to the governor's ears, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble.”
verses.kjv: And if this come to the governor’s ears, we will persuade him, and secure you.
verses.bsb: If this report reaches the governor, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble.”

verses.esv: So they took the money and did as they were directed. And this story has been spread among the Jews to this day.
verses.kjv: So they took the money, and did as they were taught: and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.
verses.bsb: So the guards took the money and did as they were instructed. And this account has been circulated among the Jews to this very day.

verses.esv: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain to which Jesus had directed them.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain Jesus had designated.

verses.esv: And when they saw him they worshiped him, but some doubted.
verses.kjv: And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.
verses.bsb: When they saw Him, they worshiped Him, but some doubted.

verses.esv: And Jesus came and said to them, “All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
verses.kjv: And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus came to them and said, “All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to Me.

verses.esv: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
verses.kjv: ¶ Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:
verses.bsb: Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit,

verses.esv: teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you. And behold, I am with you always, to the end of the age.”
verses.kjv: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world. Amen.
verses.bsb: and teaching them to obey all that I have commanded you. And surely I am with you always, even to the end of the age.”
